title: Electrical Day Technician
description: **Background:** Veolia is Ireland’s leading environmental services company. We provide a comprehensive range of energy, waste and water solutions and are dedicated to carbon reduction, protecting the environment and building the circular economy. We offer all employees a benefits package as standard which includes full VHI cover, pension and education assistance. When you see the world as we do, you see the chance to help the world take better care of its resources, and help it become a better place for everyone. It's why we're looking for someone who's just as committed as we are, to push for genuine change and bring our ambition of Ecological Transformation to life. We know that everyone here at Veolia can help us work alongside our communities, look after the environment, and contribute to our inclusive culture. **Electrical Day Technician** **Contract Duration/Type:** **Permanent** **Location: Clondalki, Dublin** **Hours:** **Monday\-Thursday 07:30 to 16:00 Friday 07:30 to 15:30** **Overview of the role:** The Lead Technicians will train on all Processes and techniques across Predictive Maintenance, CMMS (Pemac), Reliability techniques,HV and LV distribution, Turbines, Boilers Compressors, Diesel Generators Pumps, The working hours are Monday\-Thursday 07:30 to 16:00 Friday 07:30 to 15:30 The day lead technician may be required from time to time to back fill on shift, and may also be required to support the site in emergency situations outside of normal working hours. **Duties of the Role include:** * Coordinate the day to day work with shift technicians on\-site particularly morning shift changeover * Be the point of contact for service providers * Trouble\-shooting and high\-level investigation of utility plant equipment faults and failures – root cause analysis * Compliance/quality engineering – recording and investigation of deviations on utility plant and equipment * Carry out planned preventive, predictive and corrective maintenance as required * Ensure PMs are completed on time * Good knowledge and understanding of CMMS systems * Develop PMs and Job Plans as well as SOPs for plant equipment. * Liaising with site planner re stores to identify spare part requirements; * Review maintenance programme periodically to ensure optimal uptime levels are achieved * Support and comply with all Safety procedures * Experience with: HV and LV distribution. Electrical generation turbines, diesel generators, boilers and Fuel Oil storage and distribution systems. Industrial gas installations * Be able to take a leadership role in decision\-making as regards to plant operation and equipment suitability for operation; * Have sufficient knowledge of the plant to allow decision making in terms of taking plant off line, communicating with relevant people on site and be capable of identifying the action required to get plant back on line or introduce back\-up and standby systems; * Understand the actions to be taken in the event of a serious service outage; * Provide leadership and mentoring to the team in developing their capability to resolve complex plant deviations and failures * Provide leadership and mentoring to team members in developing their capability to resolve complex plant deviations and failures * Support the Site engineer \& HV Engineer in carrying out Root Cause Analysis on plant failures to determine the sequence of events, the underlying root cause and assist the in developing proposals to remedy the fault; * Provide support to capital improvement projects on utilities systems, and be involved in the commissioning and hand over from projects to operations. * Assist the Site Engineer \& HV Engineer in planning and executing complex utility service isolations for maintenance, projects and client works; * Identify, develop and, if approved, implement Continuous Improvement measures to improve the efficiency of plant and working practices; * Seek to continuously improve knowledge and understanding of complex mechanical systems; **Required skills for this role:** * Analytical mindset * Experience with six sigma/lean preferred * Previous experience required, craft qualified electrician * Past working experience ideally gained in energy conversion and distribution **Please note:** If you are an EU/EEA national, you will be asked to show proof of right to work in Ireland. If you are a non\-EU/EEA national , you will require current and valid permission to work and reside in the Republic of Ireland. Information on legislation and guides to the procedures in relation to obtaining greencard permits ,work permits, spousal/dependant permits is available on the Department of Jobs, Enterprise and Innovation website.

title: Electrical Designer Coordinator
description: **Summary** We’re seeking an experienced Electrical BIM professional to lead the delivery of electrical models for a major pharmaceutical manufacturing facility. This role is ideal for someone confident in technical ownership and coordination across multiple disciplines, ensuring accuracy, constructability, and compliance throughout the project lifecycle. **Location and Working Pattern** Location: Dublin Working Pattern: \[Remote / Hybrid / On\-site – to be confirmed] **Responsibilities** * Lead the development, management, and governance of electrical BIM models in Revit in line with project BIM Execution Plans, client standards, and industry best practice * Model complex electrical systems including containment, power distribution, equipment layouts, earthing, and lighting for pharmaceutical facilities * Deliver fully coordinated layouts and construction\-ready electrical drawings supporting all project phases * Ensure model accuracy, robustness, audit readiness, and compliance with data management requirements * Coordinate electrical models with mechanical, HVAC, process, architectural, automation, and structural disciplines in a federated BIM environment * Execute and manage clash detection and resolution using Navisworks or similar tools * Interpret electrical schematics, SLDs, layouts, and vendor documentation into accurate BIM outputs * Act as BIM interface for electrical engineering, liaising with teams, vendors, and contractors * Mentor junior BIM modellers, ensuring best practice and promoting workflow improvements **Must\-have Requirements** \[Essential qualifications, BIM/Revit/NAVISWORKS experience – insert details when confirmed] **Nice\-to\-have Skills** \[Any additional tools, sector experience, or certifications – insert details when confirmed] **Apply Now** If you have the experience to lead electrical BIM delivery for complex projects and want to make an impact, get in touch with us today to apply.

title: Electrical Maintenance Engineer | Datacentre | Dublin
description: This is an opportunity to join a forward\-thinking, globally backed energy solutions business that is actively shaping the future of sustainable power. Working at the forefront of renewable energy, grid\-scale infrastructure, and mission\-critical environments, you'll be part of a company investing heavily in both technology and people. 🔧 What you'll be doing Leading all HV switching activities and acting as the site's electrical authority Managing and coordinating planned \& reactive maintenance in a critical environment Driving performance, reliability, and continuous improvement across plant operations Acting as Subject Matter Expert (SME) for all electrical systems on site Overseeing contractors and vendors, ensuring safety, quality, and compliance Supporting root cause analysis, fault finding, and system optimisation Playing a key role in projects, upgrades, and operational strategy 🌱 Why join this company? 📈 Clear progression pathways into senior leadership and management roles ⚡ Exposure to cutting\-edge energy infrastructure (renewables, grid\-scale assets, critical systems) 🧠 A culture that values technical expertise and promotes from within 🌍 Be part of a business driving the transition to a greener, more sustainable energy future 🤝 Work in a high\-performing, collaborative team where your input genuinely matters ✅ What we're looking for Proven experience as a Senior Authorised Person (SAP) Strong background in HV switching \& electrical maintenance Experience working in critical or high\-availability environments Confident managing contractors and technical stakeholders Familiarity with SCADA / CMMS systems Electrical qualification (trade or engineering level) 💡 The opportunity: If you're looking for a role where you can step up, take ownership, and fast\-track your career, this is one of the strongest opportunities in the market right now. Adecco Ireland is acting as an Employment Agency in relation to this vacancy.

title: Entrepreneurship Instructor (AI Training)
description: **About The Role** We're looking for experienced entrepreneurship educators and business experts to help evaluate and improve AI systems trained on startups, innovation, and business planning. Your real\-world knowledge will directly shape how AI understands and communicates the principles of building and scaling businesses — making a meaningful impact on the next generation of entrepreneurship tools. * Organization: Alignerr (Powered by Labelbox) * Type: Hourly / Task\-Based Contract * Location: Remote * Commitment: 10–40 hours/week **What You'll Do** * Review AI\-generated startup scenarios, business plans, and entrepreneurship content for accuracy and practical relevance * Assess the quality of business models, financial assumptions, and go\-to\-market strategies * Identify unrealistic projections, flawed reasoning, or gaps in early\-stage decision\-making logic * Provide structured, actionable feedback to help AI produce more reliable and grounded business guidance * Work independently and asynchronously on your own schedule **Who You Are** * 3\+ years of experience teaching entrepreneurship, small business, or startup strategy * Strong understanding of business models, venture stages, and early\-stage decision\-making * Able to critically evaluate written business plans and identify what's missing or misleading * Comfortable delivering clear, applied, real\-world feedback at scale * Self\-motivated and reliable in an asynchronous remote environment **Nice to Have** * MBA or entrepreneurship\-focused graduate degree * Experience as a founder, startup advisor, or angel investor * Familiarity with AI tools, content evaluation workflows, or edtech platforms **Why Join Us** * Work on cutting\-edge AI projects with top research labs * Fully remote and flexible — work on your own schedule * Freelance perks: autonomy, variety, and global collaboration * Contribute to meaningful work that improves how AI understands business and entrepreneurship * Potential for ongoing work and contract extension

title: Executive Recruiter (Bangkok Based, Relocation Provided)
description: **About Agoda** At Agoda, we bridge the world through travel. Our story began in 2005, when two lifelong friends and entrepreneurs, driven by their passion for travel, launched Agoda to make it easier for everyone to explore the world. Today, we are part of Booking Holdings \[NASDAQ: BKNG], with a diverse team of over 7,000 people from 90 countries, working together in offices around the globe. Every day, we connect people to destinations and experiences, with our great deals across our millions of hotels and holiday properties, flights, and experiences worldwide. No two days are the same at Agoda. Data and technology are at the heart of our culture, fueling our curiosity and innovation. If you’re ready to begin your best journey and help build travel for the world, join us. **Get to Know our Team:** The People Team is a purveyor of opportunity, searching the globe for the most talented individuals and offering them an open, collaborative workplace. By prioritizing skill and potential, we have cultivated a powerful assembly of professionals through our drive for equal opportunity and diversity. We make the move to Agoda a breeze with assisted onboarding programs, and we continue to support and enrich our thousands of Agoda employees through individual growth with outstanding learning programs and various means of assistance. Our development of incredible benefits has ensured everyone can stay strong, healthy, and happy during their time at Agoda. Leading ambitious changes and making a positive impact in the lives of our employees, the People Team is a crucial and rewarding part of the Agoda family. **The Opportunity** We are seeking a **Executive Recruiter** to play a critical role in shaping the company’s leadership future. In this role, you will partner closely with senior executives and business leaders to attract, assess, and close exceptional executive\-level talent that drives long\-term growth and organizational success across the organization. This is a highly strategic position where you will operate as a trusted advisor to leadership, owning complex and confidential searches for senior and executive roles. Beyond execution, you will influence hiring strategy, provide deep market and talent intelligence, and help strengthen executive pipelines and succession planning. If you thrive in ambiguity, enjoy operating at the intersection of talent and business strategy, and bring strong executive presence, this role offers the opportunity to make a lasting impact. This role is based in Bangkok, Relocation Support will be provided. **In This Role, You’ll Get To** **Conduct Executive Search \& Assessment** * Partner with senior leadership to define executive hiring needs aligned to business priorities, growth plans, and organizational design. * Lead end\-to\-end executive search processes, including direct sourcing, market mapping, assessment, and offer management for senior and complex roles. * Build and maintain long\-term executive talent pipelines to support future leadership and succession needs. * Conduct rigorous, capability\- and values\-based executive assessments to ensure strong leadership effectiveness and cultural alignment. * Navigate highly confidential searches, evolving requirements, and complex offer scenarios with discretion and sound judgment. **Analyze Market \& Talent Intelligence** * Provide real\-time insights on executive talent markets, competitor intelligence, and senior talent availability. * Advise leaders on role scope, leveling, and compensation considerations using data and market benchmarks. * Partner with stakeholders to anticipate future leadership needs and proactively shape executive talent strategies. **Build Partnership \& Influence** * Act as a trusted advisor and coach to senior leaders on executive interviewing, assessment, and decision\-making. * Influence hiring strategy, timelines, and trade\-offs with credibility, data, and a strong understanding of business context. * Operate confidently in ambiguous or newly created roles, helping leaders bring clarity and alignment to executive hiring decisions. * Lead or contribute to recruitment initiatives and projects that elevate executive hiring standards across the organization. **What you’ll Need to Succeed:** * 8\+ years of recruiting experience in a fast\-growing tech company or top\-tier agency * Proven experience hiring senior leaders and executives in complex, fast\-growing, or scaling environments. * Excellent communication and interpersonal skills, with proven ability to take initiative and build strong, productive relationships. * Background in executive search firms and/or in\-house executive talent acquisition. * Strong track record of closing senior\-level candidates and navigating complex, high\-stakes offer negotiations. * Demonstrated ability to work autonomously with high levels of judgment, discretion, and accountability. **It's Great if you have:** * Knowledge of recruiting and/or sourcing across regional or global scale * Experience hiring across departments for Executive title: Experience Coordinator
description: At EPIC, every visitor interaction matters. From the moment guests arrive, our teams create welcoming, engaging and memorable experiences that bring Ireland’s story to life. Through exhibitions, events, genealogy and storytelling, we connect visitors with Ireland’s history, culture and global influence in meaningful ways. We’re looking for an **Experience Coordinator** to be a visible and confident frontline leader across our public\-facing spaces \- supporting Visitor Experience, Retail, Events and the Irish Family History Centre (IFHC). This role is ideal for someone who thrives in a fast\-paced environment, enjoys leading people on shift, and has a genuine interest in history, emigration, cultural programming and visitor engagement. **About the Role** The Experience Coordinator is a hands\-on frontline leadership role responsible for delivering exceptional visitor experiences during assigned shifts. You will support teams across departments, oversee daily operations, resolve issues in real time, and ensure EPIC’s standards of service, presentation and professionalism are consistently upheld. This is a part\-time position of approximately 20 hours per week, with shifts primarily scheduled across weekends, evenings and peak visitor periods. **What you’ll do** **Shift Leadership \& Visitor Experience** * Act as the lead point of contact during assigned shifts, ensuring smooth day\-to\-day operations * Support Visitor Experience, Retail, Events and IFHC teams across all public\-facing areas * Create a welcoming, engaging and well\-organised visitor environment * Monitor visitor flow and support busy periods, group visits, VIP experiences and public events * Act as a visible floor leader, engaging with visitors and responding to issues in real time * Support opening and closing procedures when required **Team Support \& Coordination** * Provide on\-the\-floor guidance, support and direction to frontline teams * Ensure staffing is coordinated effectively across operational areas * Lead by example on presentation, professionalism and service standards * Support onboarding, training and coaching activities where needed * Foster a positive, collaborative and visitor\-focused culture **Operations, Safety \& Standards** * Respond calmly and professionally to operational challenges, visitor issues or escalations * Liaise with Facilities, Security, Tech and Management teams as required * Ensure all public\-facing spaces are operational, safe and presentation\-ready * Support incident response, reporting and emergency procedures when necessary * Monitor stock levels of operational materials (audio guides, passports, earpieces, etc.) and escalate issues promptly * Ensure Health \& Safety and operational procedures are followed consistently **Commercial Awareness \& Continuous Improvement** * Support commercial goals through confident visitor engagement, ticketing awareness and retail promotion * Assist with operational reporting, handovers and daily updates for management * Identify opportunities to improve processes, visitor flow and service delivery * Support implementation of new initiatives, operational plans and company goals **What you bring** **Experience** * 2\+ years’ experience in a similar visitor\-facing, hospitality, events or cultural coordination role * Previous team leader or shift leadership experience in a fast\-paced operational environment * Experience in museums, tourism, heritage, events or public programming environments is highly desirable **Skills \& Strengths** * Warm, confident communicator with strong people and leadership skills * Calm under pressure with excellent judgement and problem\-solving ability * Organised and capable of managing multiple priorities simultaneously * Proactive, solutions\-focused and comfortable taking ownership during shifts * Collaborative and supportive team player with strong operational awareness * Genuine interest in Irish history, emigration, storytelling and public engagement * Passion for delivering exceptional visitor experiences **Apply for the job!** If you enjoy leading from the floor, supporting engaging public experiences, and helping visitors connect with Ireland’s story in meaningful ways \- we’d love to hear from you Could this be you? If so, then we would love to hear from you! 1\. Please send your CV and a short cover letter, telling us why you would like to work with us and why you think you would be a good fit for the role. *We are proud to be an equal opportunity employer and welcome applications from individuals of all backgrounds, regardless of race, ethnicity, gender, sexual orientation, age, religion, disability, or any other protected characteristic. We strive to create an environment that embraces and celebrates the differences among our employees, promoting a culture of fairness, equality, and mutual respect.*

title: Financial Reporting, Off
description: **Who we are looking for:** Based in the Dublin office, you will be part of a team of 200\+ dedicated financial reporting professionals who are focused on delivering interim and annual US GAAP/IFRS financial statements to a diverse group of alternative investment managers. **Why this role is important to us:** The team you will be joining is a part of State Street Global Services (SSGS). SSGS gives asset owners and managers access to the essential financial tools they need to deliver effective investment solutions. From core custody, accounting, fund administration and shareholder recordkeeping, to complete operations solutions and servicing for alternative assets like OTC derivatives, private equity and real estate, SSGS’ tools help our clients make better investment choices and act on growth opportunities. Join us if making your mark in the financial services industry from day one is a challenge you are up for. **What you will be responsible for:** As a Financial Reporting Officer you will Plan, schedule, and manage the production of interim / annual financial statements and daily workflow for multiple alternative investment client relationships and product types (hedge, private equity, and real estate funds). * Act as the primary point of contact for clients with responsibility for implementing and supporting the service requirements of the various client relationships serviced within the group. * Perform technical reviews of hedge, private equity, and real estate funds’ financial statements in accordance with relevant GAAP and regulatory requirements. * Set individual and team targets and priorities; maintain high standards of client service; conduct performance appraisals and give feedback on individual and team performance. * Be a source of technical support and guidance, including reviewing financial statements prepared by your teams and ensuring that all financial statements are accurate and prepared in accordance with the relevant GAAP prior to issuance to external parties (clients / auditors). * Support new business integration and ensure operating models are designed to deliver best of breed service to clients leveraging global footprint as necessary. * Manage your own continued professional development, through engaging in the performance review process and attending relevant training as per agreed training curriculum. * Deputise for your assistant vice president as required from time to time. * Perform other duties as required from time to time. **What we value** These skills will help you succeed in this role * Excellent communicate skills * Strong leadership abilities. * Excellent attention to detail * Strong knowledge of accounting standards as they relate to funds (IFRS, Irish GAAP in particular) * Ability to multi\-task and be comfortable with managing difficult situations as they arise. * Be able to communicate effectively with colleagues, clients, auditors at all levels. * Experience of working to deadlines. * Ability to delegate work to others. * Ability to effectively represent the team to clients and internal stakeholders **Education \& Preferred Qualifications** * Possess a degree in finance, business / accounting or related discipline, * Have 6\-8 years’ experience working in a similar environment or related environment, including experience managing staff * The role would also suit someone who is a qualified professional accountant (eg. ACA, ACCA, CPA). * The candidate should possess strong knowledge of US GAAP/IFRS investment company financial statements with previous experience servicing alternative investments.. **Disposition** * Self\-motivated * Good initiative * Professional * Multi\-tasking ability * Enthusiastic * Adaptable – ability to develop new approaches to solving problems * Flexible * Strong inter\-personal skills **About State Street** **What we do.** State Street is one of the largest custodian banks, asset managers and asset intelligence companies in the world. title: Financial Services Internal Auditor
description: Dublin **About RSM** RSM Ireland is one of the country’s fastest growing advisory firms, providing audit, tax and consulting services to leading domestic and international clients. From 2026, we are part of a newly formed transatlantic partnership uniting the UK, US, Canadian and Irish firms, creating a highly integrated global platform with deep banking and insurance expertise. This unique combination of scale and sector specialism enables us to deliver market‑leading insights and outcomes for our clients. We are seeking a skilled and energetic Financial Services Internal Auditor to join our growing team. In this role, you’ll play an essential part in advancing the success of our Risk and Compliance team. When you join RSM you’ll have a world of opportunity to build the career you want. It’s your future. Own it at RSM. **About The Role** RSM Ireland is seeking a Senior Associate/Consultant to join our rapidly expanding Financial Services Internal Audit team. You will work with leading banks, insurers, payment firms and asset managers, delivering high‑quality assurance and advisory services across the sector. The role offers genuine scope to carve out your own area of expertise within a fast‑growing, ambitious team, with unparalleled progression opportunities as the practice scales. You will lead fieldwork, support planning and reporting, and contribute to risk‑based internal audit programmes aligned to regulatory expectations and industry best practice. You will also be part of a close‑knit, supportive team with a proactive, outward‑facing mindset \- winning work across financial services, engaging with clients and regulators, and representing the firm at industry events, conferences and forums as a future leader in your field. **Responsibilities Include** * Deliver internal audits across financial services clients, including governance, risk, compliance, and operational areas. * Lead fieldwork and supervise junior team members. * Support audit planning, risk assessments, and scoping. * Evaluate control design and operating effectiveness. * Draft clear, insightful reports with practical recommendations. * Liaise with client stakeholders and manage relationships. * Contribute to methodology development and sector insights. * Stay current on regulatory developments (e.g. PCF oversight, CBI expectations, outsourcing, conduct risk). **The Person** The successful candidate will ideally have the following skills and qualifications * ACA, ACCA, CIA, CAMS, CISA or equivalent qualification/ working towards the qualification/ willing to undertake the exams. * Previous financial services experience or consulting experience working under EU or UK regulation is a requirement. * 2\-4 years’ experience in internal audit or risk assurance within financial services. * Excellent communication and report\-writing skills. **Nice To Have** * Enthusiasm for the role and self starter personality. * Strong understanding of governance, risk, and control frameworks * Experience supervising audit framework and mentoring junior staff. * Previous consulting or inhouse Internal Audit experience is a plus. *RSM is an equal opportunity employer.*

title: Fire & Security Apprentice
description: **Launch Your Career with Siemens!** **Fire \& Security Systems Apprentice (SOLAS)** **Location:** Midlands/Munster **Job Type:** Apprenticeship (Full\-time) **Apprenticeship Authority:** SOLAS **Start Date:** Summer 2026 **Duration:** 4 years **Why Siemens?** This isn't just a job, it's a journey. You'll be part of a vibrant early career's community, solving real business challenges, learning from experts, and growing every day. We care about your success, your wellbeing, and your future. Join us at Siemens and kickstart your career in a place where innovation meets inclusion. Whether you're passionate about tech, people, sustainability, or strategy — we've got a role that will challenge and inspire you. **What Will Your Day Look Like?** You'll be based across the Midlands/Munster region, working alongside experienced engineers. This SOLAS\-approved apprenticeship offers structured training, hands\-on experience, and a nationally recognised qualification. Your role will include: * Assisting with the installation of fire alarm, intruder alarm, CCTV, and access control systems. * Supporting testing, commissioning, and fault\-finding activities. * Carrying out routine servicing and preventative maintenance. * Installing cabling, containment, and system components. * Learning to read drawings, schematics, and technical documentation. * Adhering to all health \& safety procedures on\-site. * Maintaining accurate job and service records. * Attending and completing all required SOLAS off\-the\-job training phases. **What We're Looking For** We welcome applicants from all backgrounds; if you're curious, motivated, and ready to learn, we want to hear from you. * Leaving Certificate or equivalent (Maths and English preferred). * Strong interest in electrical, electronic, or security systems. * Practical, hands\-on approach to work. * Good communication and teamwork skills. * Reliable, punctual, and eager to learn. * Comfortable working at heights and on construction sites. * Full Driving License Essential. * Safe Pass and Manual Handling are an advantage but not essential What's In It For You? * Fully supported SOLAS apprenticeship. * On\-the\-job training with experienced technicians. * Nationally recognised qualification. * Long\-term career opportunities in a growing industry. * 26 days paid holiday \+ 5 volunteering days. * Discounts on everyday spending. * Subsidised gym memberships. **What Makes Our Process Different?** **No CV Bias:** We're changing the game. Your CV will need to be attached but won't be seen by the hiring team until the final stage of the recruitment process. We focus on potential, not past experience. **Belonging Transforms** We are fully committed to providing equal opportunities and building an inclusive workplace where a broad range of backgrounds and perspectives thrive. We embrace the many ways people think, learn, and experience the world because we know that diverse minds drive innovation. If your application makes it to the assessment stage of our process, please reach out to the recruiter who has been in contact with you and let us know of any requirements you have. **Ready to Shape the Future?** We receive lots of applications and may close our roles early as a result of this, so we really encourage you to apply as soon as possible. The future is exciting at Siemens, and we'd love you to be part of it. Apply now and start your journey with us!

title: Fitness Coach - New Club Opening
description: **Company Description** With great earning potential and the opportunity to work within a fast paced, exciting and inspiring environment we are looking for a minimum of Level 3 qualified Personal Trainers (or CIMPSA Practioner certified) to work within our Everlast Gyms to continue to help our members find their ‘Greatness from within’. As an Everlast Personal Trainer you will work as part of a great team who help take our members from their ‘good’ to their ‘Great’. With a readymade client base, state of the art facility, best in class support and development, group training products and a rapidly growing brand, there is no better place to build your business and progress in your fitness career **The Benefits** As an Everlast Personal Trainer you will: * Keep 100% of your Personal Training Income * Supportive gym floor paid hours whilst you build your business * Have dedicated marketing to assist your business services * Gain additional member facing opportunities with our signature fitness products including Group Training classes * Showcase your services and skills as PT via our exclusive member onboarding program, MOVE * Access to our brand partner discounts and offers towards your education and future development * Take advantage of our CIMPSA academy partnership to gain yearly CPD’s * Receive full training from our internal Academy and development programs * Monthly catch ups and business mentorship from your Fitness Manager * Receive a discounted rate on your Personal Training Rent whilst employed as a Fitness Coach * Work for the UK’s fearless and most ground breaking fitness brand. Job Description We have various agreement options available with a minimum of 4 hours per week based on your industry experience and career goals. These regular paid gym hours allow you to build a successful client base through the delivery of MOVE sessions, involvement in gym events and challenges, group training classes and general facility customer service duties. Our teams have a big focus on supporting new member sales and member retention as well as supporting the wider team on secondary products that drive profitability. We require our teams to deliver constantly high standards of cleanliness and presentation at all times when representing our brand. If you grow you client base rapidly, you can drop your gym floor hours to maximise your PT earning potential. **Qualifications** * Level 3 Personal Trainer Qualification * CIMSPA membership certificate ‘Practioner’ Level * Public Liability Insurance Additional Information Want to know see more of the great things our brand does please visit our LinkedIn page here. An opportunity like this at Frasers Group is for the fearless. The potential is massive, the experience unrivalled. To be able to make the most of it you need to live and breathe our principles: * Think without limits \- Think fast, think fearlessly and take the team with you * Own it and back yourself \- own the basics, own your role and own the results * Be relevant \- Relevant to our people, our partners and the planet

title: Freelance HYROX Performance Coach (HPC)
description: **Company Description** Everlast Gyms is opening its newest **HYROX Performance Centre in Dublin**, and we're looking for elite freelance HYROX Coaches to be part of this landmark launch. With strong earning potential and the opportunity to work within a fast\-paced, high\-performance environment, this is your chance to join the **UK \& Ireland's only HYROX Performance Centres** from day one in our flagship Dublin club. We offer a premium coaching environment designed to prepare members for HYROX race performance, from first race to competitive level. Our HPC model is built on: * Structured, performance\-led programming * Elite coaching standards * A results\-driven member journey As an Everlast HYROX Coach in Dublin, you will play a key role in delivering world\-class group training experiences that drive measurable outcomes and prepare members for race day, while helping us establish the HPC standard in the Irish market **Job Description** The Opportunity Join the founding coaching team at Dublin's first and only HYROX Performance Centre. You will deliver high\-quality HYROX classes within a structured, centrally governed programme, working as part of a team aligned to a unified HPC coaching philosophy. This is not a traditional freelance role. This is a performance coaching environment with clear standards, accountability, and development pathways. As a founding coach, you'll be instrumental in shaping the culture, community, and member experience of the Dublin HPC. * Be part of the launch team for Ireland's first HYROX Performance Centre * Deliver classes within the UK \& Ireland's only HYROX Performance Centres * Access a readymade, performance\-focused member base * Be part of a nationally standardised, elite coaching system * Receive ongoing education and development through HPC coaching modules and seminars * Work within a structured programming model (no need to design sessions independently) * Benefit from a competitive hourly rate plus performance\-based top\-up * Gain exposure to events, simulations, and brand activations * Be part of the UK \& Ireland's most progressive fitness brand **Key Responsibilities** * Deliver high\-energy, technically sound HYROX classes aligned to HPC standards * Coach members across all levels, from entry\-level to competitive athletes * Apply clear coaching cues, movement standards, and performance intent * Create an environment focused on effort, accountability, and progression * Ensure all sessions follow the central HPC programming and periodisation model * Deliver sessions in line with the HYROX class framework (POWER / ENGINE / FOUNDATIONAL / COMPLETE / SIM) * Proactively engage members and contribute to community and retention during the critical launch phase * Maintain a professional, high\-energy coaching presence at all times * Attend required HPC training, education, and alignment sessions **Qualifications** **Minimum Expectations** * Must hold HYROX Foundation or HYROX Level 1 qualification (minimum requirement) * Must deliver under the Everlast HYROX Performance Centre coaching framework * Must adhere to centrally programmed class structure and periodisation * Must align with coaching standards set by the HPC leadership team * Must demonstrate high levels of professionalism, reliability, and energy in delivery * Must be eligible to work in Ireland **What We're Looking For** * Coaches who are performance\-driven, not just session deliverers * Strong understanding of HYROX demands and hybrid training principles * Ability to motivate, educate, and challenge members * High attention to movement quality, standards, and coaching detail * A mindset aligned to: + **OWN IT** + **BE RELEVANT** + **THINK WITHOUT LIMITS** Our Coaching Standard At Everlast Gyms, coaching is not about running a class. It's about delivering **results**. Our HYROX Coaches are expected to: * Coach with intent, not just energy * Deliver structured sessions, not random workouts * Drive progression, not participation **Additional Information** This is a rare opportunity to be part of a launch team for a market\-leading fitness product, working within a clear system, strong brand, and elite coaching community, and to help establish HYROX Performance Centre coaching in Dublin. If you're serious about coaching, performance, and delivering results, this is the standard.

title: Full Stack Developer/Frontend Developer (React .Net Typescript, Azure)
description: **Contract Role – Full Stack Developer/Frontend Developer (React .Net Typescript, Azure) – Dublin/Hybrid – 03 months** We are seeking a skilled **Full Stack Developer/Frontend Developer (React .Net Typescript, Azure)** with a strong background in designing, developing, and deploying scalable web applications using modern technologies. Proficient in .NET for backend development and React with TypeScript **(Candidates must have the legal right to work in Ireland and be currently based in Ireland)** **Role Overview:** * Job Title: **Full Stack Developer/Frontend Developer (React .Net Typescript, Azure)** * Location: **Dublin/Hybrid** * Contract Type: **03 months** * Sector: **Healthcare** . * Top skills: **React important. Hence front\-end role with some back end. React .Net Typescript, Azure Functions** * Experienced Full Stack Engineer with a strong background in designing, developing, and deploying scalable web applications using modern technologies. Proficient in .NET for backend development and React with TypeScript * Versatile and results\-driven Full Stack Engineer with extensive experience in building and maintaining scalable, high\-performance web applications using .NET for backend services and React with TypeScript for dynamic, user\-friendly front\-end development. Demonstrates deep expertise in SQL Server database design, optimization, and integration, ensuring robust data management and retrieval across enterprise\-grade systems. * Proficient in leveraging Microsoft Azure cloud services to architect and deploy secure, resilient, and cost\-effective solutions. Skilled in implementing and maintaining CI/CD pipelines using tools like Azure DevOps, enabling streamlined development workflows, automated testing, and continuous delivery. * Thrives in Agile environments, contributing effectively to sprint planning, daily stand\-ups, and retrospectives. Experienced in using Agile project management tools such as JIRA and Rally to track progress, manage tasks, and collaborate across cross\-functional teams. * Recognized for strong communication and collaboration skills, with a proven ability to work closely with product owners, designers, QA engineers, and fellow developers to deliver high\-quality software that meets business requirements. Adept at translating complex technical concepts into clear, actionable insights for both technical and non\-technical stakeholders. * Committed to clean code practices, continuous learning, and fostering a positive team culture. Passionate about solving challenging problems and delivering impactful solutions that drive user satisfaction and business success. Please feel free to contact myself – Daisy Nguyen (dnguyen@gibbsconsulting.com) at Gibbs Consulting/Atrium UK for a confidential chat to know more details about the role. Please also note: Due to the volume of applications received for positions, it will not be possible to respond to all applications and only applicants who are considered suitable for interview will be contacted.

title: Full Stack Developers
description: **Job: Full Stack Developer** **Location: Dublin, Ireland** **Working arrangement: Hybrid (3 days onsite)** **Type: Permanent** **Salary: 65,000 \- 90,000** We are looking for a talented and dedicated Full Stack Developer to help transform the financial services landscape with our innovative digital solutions. Our customers technology is helping transform a complex banking issue with an easy SAAS based solution. They are committed to enhancing the customer experience through continuous modernization of our infrastructure and operations. #### **As a Full Stack Developer you will do:** * Full Stack Development: Design, develop, and maintain scalable and high\-performance SaaS solutions. * API Development: Create and maintain RESTful APIs and microservices, ensuring seamless integration between front\-end and back\-end systems. * Workflow Automation: Implement and manage business processes using Flowable or similar BPM tools. * SaaS Platform Development: Develop and deploy SaaS solutions, ensuring they are scalable, secure, and reliable. * Code Quality \& Best Practices: Write clean, maintainable, and efficient code, following best practices for coding, testing, and version control. * Collaboration: Work closely with cross\-functional teams including product managers, designers, and other developers to deliver high\-quality products. * Troubleshooting \& Debugging: Identify and resolve technical issues across the full stack, ensuring the smooth functioning of all applications. * Continuous Improvement: Stay up\-to\-date with emerging technologies and frameworks, and continuously improve the development process. We are looking for people with the following background: * Experience: 5\+ years of experience in full stack development, with a strong focus on Angular, Java, and Spring. * Front\-End: Proficient in Angular (latest versions), with a deep understanding of reactive programming, state management, and responsive design. * Back\-End: Strong experience in Java and Spring framework, including Spring Boot, Spring Security, and Spring Data. * BPM Tools: Hands\-on experience with Flowable or similar Business Process Management tools (e.g., Camunda, Activiti). * SaaS: Proven experience in developing and deploying SaaS solutions, with knowledge of cloud platforms such as AWS, Azure, or Google Cloud. * Experience of design and build based on principles of configuration and low code implementations. * Databases: Experience with both relational (e.g., PostgreSQL, MySQL) and NoSQL (e.g., MongoDB, Cassandra) databases. * APIs: Expertise in designing and consuming RESTful APIs and knowledge of GraphQL is a plus. * Version Control: Proficiency in Git, with experience in collaborative workflows using GitHub, GitLab, or Bitbucket. * DevOps: Basic understanding of CI/CD pipelines, containerization (Docker), and infrastructure as code (e.g. Terraform) is a plus. * Cloud Platforms: Experience with cloud\-native architectures and services on AWS. * Microservices: Experience with microservices architecture and container orchestration (e.g., Kubernetes). * Agile Methodologies: Familiarity with Agile/Scrum development processes. * Testing: Experience with automated testing frameworks (e.g., JUnit, Selenium, Jasmine) and test\-driven development (TDD). * Communication: Excellent verbal and written communication skills, with the ability to articulate complex technical concepts to non\-technical stakeholders. This is an exciting opportunity to join a scaling business with a highly innovative product. The business is well funded but still in a roll the sleeves up and work together type mentality. The career potential for the business is great. If you would be interested please submit your CV now for immediate consideration.

Skilled in implementing and maintaining CI/CD pipelines using tools like Azure DevOps, enabling streamlined development workflows, automated testing, and continuous delivery. * Thrives in Agile environments, contributing effectively to sprint planning, daily stand\-ups, and retrospectives. Experienced in using Agile project management tools such as JIRA and Rally to track progress, manage tasks, and collaborate across cross\-functional teams. * Recognized for strong communication and collaboration skills, with a proven ability to work closely with product owners, designers, QA engineers, and fellow developers to deliver high\-quality software that meets business requirements. Adept at translating complex technical concepts into clear, actionable insights for both technical and non\-technical stakeholders. * Committed to clean code practices, continuous learning, and fostering a positive team culture. Passionate about solving challenging problems and delivering impactful solutions that drive user satisfaction and business success.

title: Fund Operations - Asset Servicing Administrator
description: * Full Time Permanent position * Hybrid role based in our City Centre offices **What we offer** We have embraced a hybrid working model for most of our positions, which means that you can enjoy a balanced approach of working from home for part of the week and working from the office for the remainder of the week. We offer a comprehensive benefits package including competitive salaries and bonuses, robust Learning and Development support, excellent Defined Contribution pension and comprehensive Wellbeing initiatives and support to name but a few. Further details on our benefits package can be accessed here Benefits (life\-careers.com) At Irish Life, our purpose is to help people build better futures. Our company values shape everything we do and underpin our vision of being the driving force behind Ireland’s financial, physical, and mental well\-being. We believe in doing the right thing—acting with integrity and making fair, responsible decisions. We aim high, setting ambitious goals and constantly find better ways to innovate and improve. Our customers are at the heart of everything we do, and we are dedicated to understanding their needs and exceeding their expectations. We believe that as a team we work best together, in a supportive, collaborative environment where every idea and contribution is valued. **Role Overview** We are looking to appoint an Administrator within the Income \& Corporate Events team. The successful candidate will report to the Manager of the Income \& Corporate Events Team. This team’s primary role is to ensure that all Income is accrued and received for client funds as expected; Corporate Actions entitlements are protected so that Fund values and the trading book of record is updated on a timely basis. Working as part of a small team, you will primarily be responsible for the processing, reflection, and management of all Dividend and Coupon entitlements across global markets for a variety of Fund Managers and client structures. This is an excellent opportunity for the right candidate to gain exposure to an array of varied and diverse Income events across all regions and increase their knowledge and skillset with excellent exposure to various stakeholders within the business. **Team Background** The Income and Corporate Events (ICE) Team sits within BTS in Keyridge. The team has responsibilities including protection of client entitlements around Income and Corporate action events, ensuring clients’ proxy voting intentions are followed through, and managing the control framework around SFTR reporting. **What you will help us to achieve** * Review of daily Dividend Adjustments and notification to relevant teams of associated Fund impacts * Daily substantiation and investigation into income exceptions, cross\-checking with various data sources to ensure correct income reflected. * Regular liaison with external custodians and clients in relation to fund entitlements arising from Income events. * Review and maintenance of Fund tax status and withholding rates across the various client structures, ensuring correct documentation in place with relevant Custodian. * Respond in timely manner to internal Income queries received from Fund Management and other stakeholders including Settlements, Corporate Actions, Reconciliations and Pricing. * Regular review of Risk Management procedures and Regulatory reporting requirements and associated risk to business * Continuous review of day\-to\-day processes to drive efficiencies and innovative solutions. * Ad\-hoc tasks including ensuring Proxy Voting events are reviewed and clients correctly set\-up for voting and daily Stock Lending Collateral Reporting. **What you will need to be successful in the role** * Strong understanding of securities and investment markets, with good knowledge of the regulatory framework and of Keyridge’s business. * Blend of technical ability, sound judgement, commercial awareness, and high numeracy with good IT capabilities. * Excellent problem\-solving and decision\-making skills, with a questioning attitude and ability to recognise, resolve, and appropriately escalate issues. * Strong organisational, planning, and prioritisation skills, with proven ability to work to tight deadlines and see tasks through to completion. * High level of accuracy and attention to detail. * Strong communication, interpersonal, and collaboration skills, with an ability to build and maintain effective working relationships. * Professional, enthusiastic manner with personal drive and energy to perform consistently to a high standard under pressure. * Desire and willingness to challenge and improve practices and processes. * Third\-level business qualification. **About us** Keyridge Asset Management Limited, the investment management company within Irish Life Assurance, part of Great\-West Lifeco (GWL) Group, manages assets of over €150bn. Keyridge provides investment products and solutions to both institutional and retail clients, offering a full range of active and indexed funds together with tailored investment solutions meeting specific clients objectives. The company reserves the right to draw up a shortlist as part of the selection process. Where Agency assistance is required the Irish Life Recruitment Team will engage directly with suppliers. Unsolicited CVs / profiles supplied to Irish Life by Recruitment Agencies will not be accepted for this role. Irish Life is proud to be an Equal Opportunities employer. We have created an environment of inclusion and growth to help people achieve their potential, to ensure they can bring their whole selves to work and to feel valued. We celebrate diversity of thought and perspective and want all of our employees to feel valued, respected and supported. We have built a culture that promotes equality and inclusion for all of our current and future employees and strive to ensure that our behaviours, processes and practices are aligned to this culture. If you require any accommodations during the recruitment process please contact lifecareers@irishlife.ie and we will be delighted to ensure you are fully supported to be your best. Keyridge Asset Management Limited, trading as Irish Life Investment Managers and trading as Setanta Asset Management, supports Equal Opportunity and is authorised and regulated as an investment firm by the Central Bank of Ireland.

title: GP Sentinel Network Co-Ordinator
description: **Title: GP Sentinel Network Co\-Ordinator** **Reporting to:** Medical Director **Base:** Remote **Contract type:** Service Level Agreement **Contract duration:** 12\-month **Hours per week:** One day per week from September 2026, increasing to two days from 1st January 2027 **Closing date:** Sunday 7th June **About The Irish College Of GPs** For an in\-depth insight about the College explore https://www.irishcollegeofgps.ie/ **Equal Opportunities Employer** The Irish College of GPs is an equal opportunities employer. We are committed to ensuring that all job applicants and employees receive equal treatment regardless of gender, civil status, family status, sexual orientation, religion, age, disability, race, membership of the Traveler community, or any other protected characteristic under Irish equality legislation. We are committed to making reasonable accommodations to enable applicants to participate in the job application and interview process. Please email hr@icgp.ieto discuss any accommodations required. **About The Irish Sentinel GP Surveillance Network** The Irish sentinel GP surveillance network is a collaborative project between the Irish College of GPs (the College) the Health Service Executive (HSE)\-Health Protection and Surveillance Centre (HPSC) and the National Virus Reference laboratory (NVRL) funded by the Health Service Executive (HSE). The College via a memorandum of understanding agreement with the HSE gathers data from 100 participating GP practices around the country and provides it to the HSE partners in line with the memorandum agreement. The job of the GP Sentinel Network Co\-Ordinator is a College appointment, reporting to the Medical Director and charged with managing all aspects of the College arm of the project. **Schedule of Services** * Maintenance, communication, education and support of the 100 sentinel sites * Performance monitoring and quality improvement initiatives * Recruitment of new sites when vacancies arise * Liaison with all other relevant parties including the Irish College of GPs, HPSC, NVRL, Healthlink and accredited software vendors * Ensuring the weekly data report is sent to the HPSC on time every week * Ensuring that sites are appropriately coding and swabbing relevant cases * Monitoring of returns/swabbing to validate payments due to GPs * Managing specific projects as they arise – e.g. denominator * Direct line management of administrative support staff **Person Specification** **Qualifications Experience** * Be a GP practice principal * A fully qualified GP with MICGP, current member of the College in good standing. * Be registered with the Irish Medical Council of Ireland on the Specialist Division for General Practice. * Be enrolled on and in compliance with the Irish College of GPs Professional Competence Scheme. * Must currently hold a part\-time clinical appointment in general practice in Ireland, equivalent to at least four sessions per week and you will be required to continue to meet these criteria if appointed. You must also have held such an appointment within the past two years. * Have an interest in computerisation and IT in general, including a working knowledge of digital medical records, disease coding, proficient use of excel and an interest in learning about creating, using and querying databases. * Current participation in the Sentinel GP Network is desirable but not essential. If a successful candidate is not in the network, it is expected that their practice would do so. * Strong leadership, interpersonal and communication skills. **Behavioral Competencies** Please contact Dr Michael Joyce, sentinelnetwork.lead@icgp.iefor any informal enquiries in relation to this role. *Please note that communication in respect of this position will come from our recruitment platform HireHive so please review spam folders and settings to ensure that emails from HireHive will be delivered to your email address.*

title: Golf Trader - EU
description: Working as a Golf Trader at DL Trading, you will: * Manage trading operations \- confirm data feeds are available, ensure trading models launch successfully, diagnose issues and work with the operations team to resolve * Monitor live trading \- monitor prices and trading activity during live sporting events, ensure models have accurate gamestate information, make adjustments as needed * Work with quantitative traders \- share feedback on trading activity, analyze trading performance, take on data\-related projects as needed * Customer service and customer management as needed * Other duties as assigned Skills you will need: * A strong passion for and knowledge of sports, in particular golf, is a huge plus. Ideal candidate would know who Guido Migliozzi is. * Understanding of probabilities and betting odds * Ability to navigate GUI\-based technical tools for trading operations * Schedule will be Thurs\-Sun to cover golf tournaments, with some occasional additional flexibility required * Be a self\-starter and dependable, as you will have some live trading shifts without oversight Bonus points for: * SQL, Python, and / or Linux skills * Experience with sports betting and / or trading in financial markets

title: Graduate / Part-Qualfiied Accountant
description: Downey Osborne is partnering with a well\-established chartered accountancy practice in Co. Meath to appoint a Part Qualified Accountant to join its growing team. With a strong and loyal client base built over more than 30 years, this firm offers excellent exposure across audit, accounts preparation, tax and advisory work within a supportive and collaborative environment. This opportunity will suit an ambitious part qualified accountant looking to continue developing their technical skillset within a progressive practice environment. **Benefits** * Competitive salary and firm bonus scheme * Hybrid working * Study Support * Early finish every Friday * Professional subscriptions fully funded * Free parking and easily accessible office location * Strong work life balance with limited overtime expectations **About You** * Part Qualified ACA, ACCA or CIMA accountant * Minimum 2 years’ experience within an accountancy practice environment * Strong technical accounting knowledge with good attention to detail * Excellent communication and client relationship skills * Strong organisational skills with the ability to manage deadlines * Audit exposure beneficial but not essential **About Your New Role** **Always** * Prepare and review annual financial statements for a varied client portfolio * Assist with management accounts, reconciliations and year\-end processes * Prepare company and personal tax returns along with statutory filings * Liaise directly with clients and support ongoing accounting requirements **Often** * Support year\-end audits and completion of audit\-related assignments * Assist senior team members on advisory and special client projects * Work across a broad range of industries and client types * Prepare journals, balance sheet reconciliations and financial reports **Occasionally** * Assist with process improvements and internal practice projects * Support clients with accounting software and bookkeeping queries * Contribute to ad hoc assignments across the wider practice team **Never** * Be expected to wrestle a badger out of a client’s warehouse during month\-end reporting deadlines * Be expected to explain deferred tax calculations to an angry goose Ref (35041\)

title: Graduate BIM Engineer
description: Winthrop Technologies is a dedicated data centre delivery partner providing turnkey solutions to our clients, headquartered in Dublin and delivering throughout Europe. Winthrop specialises in providing turnkey data centre solutions, providing services from design, through to construction across the full range of civil, structural, architectural, mechanical and electrical services as well as commissioning. As an engineering\-focused organisation. Winthrop has grown to become one of the leading and most trusted companies in our sector. We are currently constructing multiple turnkey data centre projects in **7 different European countries** , equating to over **430MW of IT load in flight** . **Winthrop Graduate Programme:** Building on the success of our comprehensive Graduate Programme introduced in 2022, we offer an opportunity to work on cutting\-edge projects across Ireland and Europe. This enables graduates to gain valuable technical experience during a crucial stage in their careers. Our programme is uniquely designed to develop skilled professionals with a deep understanding of all phases of large\-scale construction projects, providing graduates with exposure to the complete construction process. **Responsibilities:** Your learning journey will span the entire lifecycle of a data centre project, from initial design and building shell construction through to internal fit\-out, commissioning, and final handover. You will gain hands\-on experience in both Construction and Project Management activities including: Jobs * Develop, manage, and coordinate BIM models using Revit, Navisworks, and related platforms * Support multidisciplinary coordination and clash detection processes * Assist in implementing BIM Execution Plans (BEPs) and project BIM standards * Work closely with engineering, design, and construction teams to ensure model accuracy * Extract model data, schedules, and quantities to support project delivery * Contribute to continuous improvement of BIM workflows and digital tools **Requirements** * Bachelor's degree (or higher) in Engineering, Construction Management, Architecture, BIM, or a related discipline * Strong working knowledge of Revit * Familiarity with Navisworks and clash detection workflows * Understanding of BIM standards, workflows, and coordination principles * Strong problem\-solving and analytical skills * Excellent attention to detail and organisational skills * Ability to work collaboratively within multidisciplinary teams * Strong communication skills and a proactive mindset **Benefits** * Structured Graduate Development Programme with mentoring and training * Supportive career development and collaborative team environment within BIM * Exposure to complex, large\-scale projects and advanced BIM workflows * Competitive salary package ***Winthrop Technologies is an equal opportunities employer***

title: Graduate Business Development Executive
description: **Overview** We are seeking a motivated and ambitious Business Development Executive to join our clients growing team. based in Dundalk, this role offers hands\-on experience across multiple business functions and is an excellent opportunity for a student or recent graduate looking to gain real\-world experience in a fast\-paced environment. **Key Duties \& Responsibilities** * Identify key customers and build strong relationships to generate new sales opportunities and support year\-on\-year sales growth. * Develop and implement marketing plans to reach target audiences through social media, conferences, exhibitions, and other channels. * Engage proactively with customers to generate leads, track projects, promote new business lines, and drive sales performance. * Champion the needs of new customers and ensure a positive customer experience. * Manage the new customer sales pipeline and support incremental sales targets. * Prepare regular reports on sales and business development activities, including progress against key performance indicators (KPIs). * Gather and provide competitor intelligence to support product costing, market development, and product positioning strategies. * Provide feedback on issues that may impact customer relationships and business performance. * Collaborate with internal teams to support the development of innovative products and service offerings. * Work closely with Marketing and Product Management teams to develop campaigns for new technologies and solutions. **Qualifications \& Experience** * Currently studying or recently graduated in Marketing, Business, Communications, or a related field. * Strong written and verbal communication skills. * Excellent organisational skills with the ability to multitask and meet deadlines. * Analytical mindset with an interest in data and reporting; experience with Google Analytics or similar tools is beneficial. * Proficient in Microsoft Office applications, including Word, Excel, and PowerPoint. * Experience with CRM or CMS platforms is an advantage. ***For a confidential conversation about the role please apply for the role or contact Owen Clancy at oclancy@sterlingengineeringeu.com By applying to this job post you agree that Sterling Engineering may process your personal data for recruitment purpose***

title: Graduate Electrical Engineer
description: **Job Description** Are you an enthusiastic **Graduate Electrical Engineer** ? IPS is hiring! Join our industry\-leading team at our European office based in **Dublin, Ireland** and become part of our enriching Graduate Programme. The Graduate Electrical Engineer will undertake specific tasks on project teams using their education, creativity and expertise to resolve engineering problems and tasks as they are assigned. A pool of knowledge and experience from all of the IPS teams across the world will be available to our graduates and, consequently, more experienced staff will always be available when a graduate engineer needs support. **Job Duties And Responsibilities** * As a graduate Electrical Engineer, you will get the opportunity to work on world class projects for global clients within the Life Sciences sector. * Work with a professional team of top industry experts in the delivery of complex facilities * Participate in engineering design reviews (GMP, safety, operational, maintenance) * Use computer assisted engineering and design software and equipment to prepare engineering and design documents * Research design options and documents findings for Senior Engineer and Line Manager * Research code issues and ask general questions of code officials * Ensure that drawings and designs are in accordance with IPS engineering and design standards * Promote and apply Health and Safety in all aspects of Electrical Design and Construction * Support design computations and quantity estimates for the smaller, less complex projects within the business unit (low voltage systems, fire alarm systems, backup power generation, electrical power distribution, life safety systems, etc.) * Support with calculations using engineering formulas and skills for design such as lighting calculations, power distribution calculations, equipment sizing, etc. * Keep Project Manager informed of workload and any changes in project scope or additional services * Interface with engineers in our global network of offices * Support selecting equipment based on recommendations of Project Designer/Engineer and fills out equipment schedules. * Organise and maintain project files. Maintains a neat and organised work area which allows efficient access to information required by other team members. * Have a can\-do attitude and takes ownership of projects under the supervision of a Senior Electrical Engineer. **Qualifications \& Requirements** * Level 8 Hons Degree in Electrical Services Engineering or Electrical Engineering (preferably a Masters degree). * Proven skills in computer operation for engineering design programs, Revit and computer aided drafting. * Some previous engineering/design experience would be desirable. * Desire to work towards achieving CEng with Engineers Ireland/CIBSE/I Mech E. * Desire to contribute to the development of Electrical Engineering Team within IPS * Good interpersonal skills and demonstrates the ability to work within multi discipline team All interviews are conducted either in person or virtually, with video required. **About Us** IPS, a Berkshire Hathaway Company, is a global leader in developing innovative business solutions for the biotechnology and pharmaceutical industries. Through operational expertise and industry\-leading knowledge, skill, and passion, IPS provides consultancy services, architecture, engineering, project controls, construction management, and compliance services that allow clients to develop and manufacture life\-impacting products. Its newest acquisition, Linesight, specializes in cost, schedule, risk, program, and project management services in various market sectors, including data centers, life sciences, and high\-tech industries. With the addition of Linesight, IPS has over 3,000 professionals in over 45 offices across 17 countries in the Americas, Europe, Asia Pacific, Southeast Asia, Australia, and the Middle East. Specialties Process Architecture, Process Design, Engineering, Construction, Commissioning, Qualification, LEAN Project Delivery, Strategic Master Planning, Energy Conservation Management, Project Controls, Program/Project Management, Operations/Logistics Strategy and Planning, EPCMV and Staffing Services. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment at IPS without regard to race; creed; color; religion; national origin; sex; age; disability; sexual orientation; gender identity or expression; genetic predisposition or carrier status; veteran, marital, or citizenship status; or any other status protected by law. IPS is proud to be an affirmative action and equal opportunity employer. *Attention Search Firms / Third\-Party Recruiters: IPS is not seeking assistance or accepting unsolicited resumes from search firms for employment opportunities with IPS. Regardless of past practice, all resumes submitted by search firms to any employee at IPS via email, the Internet, or directly to hiring managers at IPS in any form without a valid written search agreement in place for a specific position will be deemed the sole property of IPS, and no fee will be paid in the event a candidate is hired by IPS as a result of the referral or through other means.* Connect With Us! If you do not meet the qualifications for this specific role, but would like to submit your resume for general consideration, please click here!

title: Graduate Program
description: **Launch Your Global Career After Graduation** **Teach English in China \| Full\-Time \| Entry\-Level Friendly** Just graduated (or graduating soon) and not ready for a desk job? Want to travel, gain international work experience, and build transferable skills while earning a competitive salary? **English 1** is hiring **recent graduates and early\-career professionals** to teach English in China. No prior teaching experience required. Full training, visa sponsorship, and structured career development are included. This is an ideal **first job** , **graduate program alternative** , or **gap\-year career launch** for ambitious, globally minded graduates. **What you'll do** * Teach English to young learners aged 3–16 in a fun, interactive, and immersive classroom setting. * Deliver high\-energy lessons using English 1’s award\-winning curriculum and digital tools. * Provide constructive feedback and help students build confidence in their English skills. * Take part in school events, cultural activities, and student engagement programs. **What you’ll get** * Competitive salary \+ performance bonus after 6 months * 21 days paid leave (10 days paid leave \+ 11 days statutory holidays) * Flight allowance * International health insurance (co\-paid) * Airport pick\-up support * \*\*Sponsored legal Z work visa \+ up to 2 weeks sponsored hotel stay * Comprehensive induction training, ongoing support, and fully prepared teaching materials * TEFL certificate \+ skill development \& career advancement * Distance learning courses (Level 6 Trinity College CertPT, DELTM) * English 1\-organized social and cultural activities \+ free Mandarin Chinese lessons *The hotel accommodation for the first two weeks will be deemed as a sponsorship upon successful completion of the first\-year contract. \*\*Sponsored legal Z work visa expenses will be reimbursed.* **Who we’re looking for** * A bachelor’s degree (in any subject) * A valid passport * A genuine interest in education, travel, and cultural exchange. * ESL certification (TEFL, TESOL, CELTA) is a plus\- we can sponsor * Clean background check. **Why work at English 1?** * Teachers are at the core of everything we do at English 1 * Over **30 years** of success in China with **200\+ schools** in **50\+ cities** . * Strong presence in **China** and **Indonesia** . * **Professional development** and career **advancement opportunities** .
