title: Graduate Quantity Surveyors 2026
description: **Graduate Quantity Surveyor 2026** **Position** : Graduate Quantity Surveyor 2026 **Location** : Dublin, Kildare, Cork, Limerick, Gavle, Copenhagen, Groningen, Charleroi, Hillerod **Start date:** We operate on a rolling recruitment basis. Please advise your availability to start in your application form. You can discuss this further with your recruiter upon application. **Company Description** We are a leading engineering services company with a proven track record of high quality, customer focused project delivery for over 130 years. Our core divisions include Mechanical Engineering, Electrical Engineering, Sprinkler and Fire Protection. Some of the other services we provide include Waste to Energy (BioEnergy), Specialist Lift, Off Site Fabrication, Technical Support Services, Maintenance, Asset Management, BIM (Building Information Modelling) and Geo\-Surveying. We offer competitive salaries, financial support for further training, Continuous Professional Development (CPD) leading to chartered status, performance incentives and the ability to progress your career with a world class engineering contractor. **Job Description** * Work closely with the Senior Quantity Surveyor as well as a multidisciplinary team of engineers and industry experts to deliver cutting\-edge projects. * Assist the team with monthly progress applications. * Generate and substantiate variations and contracts administration for all projects. * Support the team with reporting on a weekly and monthly basis. * Complete site progress walks. * Assist in production/coordination of weekly construction reports. This role will provide exposure to a wide range of expertise relating to pharmaceutical, life sciences, data centre, construction and other sectors. The successful candidate will be supported in developing their knowledge and skills as their responsibilities grow and evolve to achieve Chartered Status. **Requirements** To excel in this role, you should possess the following skills and qualifications: * Degree Qualification in Quantity Surveying/Construction/Engineering. * Valid Safepass / Manual handling certificates required for successful applicants * Experience in construction industry is advantageous, demonstrating your understanding of industry best practices and technologies. * 0\-1 years’ post qualification experience in an M\&E contracting environment. * Strong team player with excellent interpersonal skills, able to collaborate effectively with colleagues, clients, and external partners. * Excellent organisational and planning skills. * Proficiency in MS Office systems required. * Demonstrated appetite for continuous learning and personal development. **Graduate Development Programme** As part of our commitment to your growth and career development, you will be enrolled in our comprehensive 2\-year Graduate Development Programme. This programme includes experiential learning, mentoring, professional development courses, and in\-house training on key skills such as technical writing, presentation, and time management. You will benefit from peer to peer learning and receive support for obtaining professional certifications. This structured approach ensures you gain the necessary skills and experience to progress in your career. **Benefits** Be part of our Graduate Programme and contribute to meaningful projects that drive innovation and sustainability across multiple industries. We offer a competitive salary package commensurate with experience and qualifications. In addition, you will have the opportunity to work in a dynamic and supportive environment, collaborating with industry leaders and contributing to a sustainable future. Our company values work\-life balance and encourages professional growth through training and development programs. If you are ready to make a difference and be part of a growing industry, please submit your CV, along with a cover letter highlighting your relevant experience and why you are interested in this role. Recognising the contributions and respecting our people is core to our culture and values. We are an equal opportunities employer, and we encourage candidates from all backgrounds to apply for roles. Please note, we are currently not accepting applications from agencies for this role.

title: Graduate Transport Planner (Dublin)
description: Location Dublin, Dublin, Ireland Capabilities Planning and Consulting Office Setup Hybrid Job ID \#30328 Market Transportation At Jacobs, we're challenging today to reinvent tomorrow by solving the world's most critical problems for thriving cities, resilient environments, mission\-critical outcomes, operational advancement, scientific discovery and cutting\-edge manufacturing, turning abstract ideas into realities that transform the world for good. Your impact Join the Jacobs Graduate Development Programme (GDP) and have a meaningful, positive impact on our environments, communities and societies Bring your passion, your ingenuity and your vision. Let’s see the impact we can create, together. **What will you get?** Our Graduate Development Programme is a structured two\-year scheme designed to support your development at the start of your Jacobs career journey. Through a combination of blended learning, mentoring, progress reviews and networking, you will establish yourself as knowledgeable and competent across four key focus areas; human skills, consulting skills, champion our strategy and your passions. The Graduate Development Programme allows you to customise your learning journey to reflect your specific interests, career goals, and technical skillset – creating a graduate experience that is unique to you. The GDP is an experience programme, meaning that a large portion of what you’ll learn will be on\-the\-job. You’ll work with your manager and mentor to collect the learning experiences that will be most meaningful for you. The programme is more than just a steppingstone. It’s a launchpad for your career, offering you the tools, experiences and support you need. Join us and unlock your potential as you work on meaningful projects and collaborate with industry leaders to deliver outcomes and solutions for complex challenges. **About the opportunity** The Graduate Transport Planners/Engineers will join our Transport Planning and Mobility Solutions team which is part of our Cities \& Places business unit. We can create thriving places across different scales: from regions to cities, neighbourhoods to sites, by offering an integrated design approach. Our business unit brings together transport planners, engineers, architects, designers, surveyors, urban designers and project managers to provide collaborative and coordinated value design solutions for our clients both nationally and internationally. Our team skillsets cover the full remit of transport planning across all modes; from high level policy and strategy advice through transport modelling/appraisal to active travel, streets design, traffic engineering and junction design. We are interested in hearing from candidates who are keen to develop a rounded set of skills, as well as those who have more specialised interests including but not limited to traffic engineering, active travel scheme design and transformational placemaking, transport decarbonisation, traffic management solutions or public transport schemes for example. You will have the support of a mentor, our management team and experts who will help guide your progress. You will also be given the opportunity to pursue relevant business and technical training to support your career development, as well as room to grow your network and knowledge base to create innovative solutions. We are looking for aspirational Graduate Transport Planners/Engineers to join our teams in Ireland and the UK. This opportunity is for a role in our Dublin office – other locations are available at https://careers.jacobs.com/en\_US/careers The start date for this position is September 2026\. Here's what you'll need * Be working towards a Degree or Master’s level qualification in Transport Planning, Geography, Economics, Mathematics/Statistics, Urban Planning, Civil Engineering, Physics, Natural Sciences or Computer/Data Science * Hold a keen interest in transport planning and/or traffic engineering topics, such as: healthy streets, walking, cycling and public transport planning, transport policy/strategy, transport modelling/appraisal, junction improvement, placemaking etc. * A flexible self\-starter who is keen to drive your career forward. * Adaptable to change, with a thirst for learning new things. * You must have the permanent right to work in Ireland. **Selection Process** * Step 1: Apply Online * Step 2: Solo Video Assessment * Step 3: Interview **What else we provide** * A flexible workstyle, embracing a hybrid working pattern and supporting those who need to balance work/life commitments a bit differently than others. * Reimbursement towards relevant professional development and memberships. * A focus on safety, health, and wellbeing, prioritising our people through a culture of caring and positive mental health champion network. * Opportunities to give back to our communities through our Collectively programme, incorporating matched\-funding, paid volunteering time and charitable donations. \#EarlyCareersIreland \#GraduateIreland \#GraduateJob2026 \#GraduateCareersIreland \#GraduaterecruitmentIreland \#GraduateJobsIreland \#GraduateopportunitiesIreland Joining Jacobs not only connects you locally but globally. Our values stand on a foundation of safety, integrity, inclusion and belonging. We put people at the heart of our business, and we truly believe that by supporting one another through our culture of caring, we all succeed. With safety and flexibility always top of mind, we’ve gone beyond traditional ways of working so you have the support, means and space to maximize your potential. You’ll uncover flexible working arrangements, benefits, and opportunities, from well\-being benefits to our global giving and volunteering program, to exploring new and inventive ways to help our clients make the world a better place. No matter what drives you, you’ll discover how you can cultivate, nurture, and achieve your goals – all at a single global company. Find out more about life at Jacobs. We aim to embed inclusion and belonging in everything we do. We know that if we are inclusive, we’re more connected and creative. We accept people for who they are, and we champion the richness of different perspectives, lived experiences and backgrounds in the workplace, as a source of learning and innovation. We are committed to building vibrant communities within Jacobs, including through our Jacobs Employee Networks, Communities of Practice and our Find Your Community initiatives, allowing every employee to find connection, purpose, and belonging. title: HR Generalist
description: At Securitas Security Services Ireland, the human resources (HR) team is focused on what people need and how to provide it. For a people person who wants to begin a career in the exciting world of HR, this is the best place to dive in. We are searching for a qualified and resourceful Human Resources Generalist to support our department in ensuring smooth and efficient business operations. The Human Resources Generalist will have both administrative and strategic responsibilities and will help with important various important functions within the H.R. department. We understand that our business thrives when our employees thrive, and it begins with hiring the right Human Resources Generalist. **Are you ready to join a fantastic team!** 📍 **Dublin 15** 🕒 **Monday to Friday, 9\.00am \- 5\.30pm (Hybrid Role)** 💰 **€55,000 \- €60,000 DOE** Why Join Us? * Be part of a company that lives by its values: Integrity, Vigilance, and Helpfulness * Career development opportunities and industry\-recognised training * A supportive team environment * Competitive salary and benefits package * Make a meaningful impact every day Join Securitas and help us **“See a Different World.”** Apply now and become a key player in delivering safety and peace of mind. **Job Description** * Assist in developing and executing personnel procedures and policies, and provide guidance and interpretation for business operations. * Participate in development of HR objectives and systems, including metrics, queries, and ongoing reports for company requirements. * Support with case file coordination relevant to WRC hearings, within agreed timeframes. * Assist in administering benefits, compensation, and employee performance programs. * Suggest new procedures and policies for improving employee experience as well as the efficiency of HR department and company. * Ensure compliance with local and national regulations and applicable employment laws and update policies and procedures when necessary. * Prepare paperwork and schedules for smooth new\-hire onboarding process, coordinating with cross\-functional departments to deliver an exceptional first\-day experience. * Support all administrative tasks for onboarding, new\-hire orientations, and exit interviews, including data entry in human resources information systems (HRIS) and audits for accuracy and compliance. * Provide a dedicated and effective HR advisory service to employees that covers absence and health issues, conduct and capability, grievances, organizational change, and all other employee\-relations matters. * Be the primary backup for payroll processing, including biweekly and monthly updates to employee files, bonus/incentive pay, vacation/sick pay, expense reimbursements, hourly\-employee validations, and benefits changes. * Assist in the communication, interpretation, and upkeep of employee handbook, employee directory, and organizational chart, and contribute to policy development. * Managing employee complaints, managing grievance procedures, and supporting counselling with other stakeholders. * Investigating and resolving complex or critical employee relations issues in a timely and effective manner. * Manage the dedicated HR email account. * Any other ad hoc duties as requested by your Line Manager. **Qualifications** * Excellent communication and interpersonal skills, ethics, and cultural awareness * Aptitude for problem\-solving and thorough knowledge of HR procedures and policies * Advanced knowledge of HRIS and ability to learn new technical systems, when necessary * Detail\-oriented with a commitment to HR and employee relations * Collaborative approach with a focus on professional development. * Ability to determine courses of action based upon detailed written instructions. * Bachelor’s degree (or equivalent) in human resources, business, or related field * Proven success working in a HR department * Resourceful mindset and strong attention to detail * Knowledge of Microsoft software * Knowledge of national laws and regulations related to employment law * Excellent organisational, communication, and interpersonal abilities * CIPD Qualification * Must have 3\-5 years experience in Employee Relations * Demonstrated experience in representing and attending the WRC on behalf of the organisation. **Additional Information** At Securitas, we believe in rewarding our people for their hard work. We offer a range of great benefits designed to support our people and their families. These comprehensive benefits include discounted health care scheme, company pension, bike to work scheme, access to hundreds of discounts from well\-known brands and retailers, access to hundreds of high\-quality online education courses from some of the world’s leading learning institutions, ways to save for the future, and other resources to improve health and well\-being. **Benefits** * Substantial Maternity Leave top up payment * 24 hour EAP (Employee Assistance Programme) free confidential counselling and advice supporting our employees in a number of areas. * Employer Contribution Pension Scheme from the day you join the Company * Progression, training and development opportunities * Christmas Savings Scheme * Discounted Private Healthcare Scheme (Cash back on everyday medical costs, such as dental check\-ups, optical, GP and Emergency Department visits.) * Internal recognition schemes * Bicycle to work scheme from the day you join the Company (Save up to 52% on a bike and accessories) *Securitas is an equal opportunities Employer* P.S.A. Licence Number : 00352 Website: https://www.securitas.ie/ Available jobs: https://www.securitas.ie/careers/available\-jobs/ Benefits at Securitas: https://www.securitas.ie/careers/benefits\-at\-securitas/ Securitas Integrity Line: https://www.securitas.ie/sustainability/securitas\-integrity\-line/

title: Healthcare Assistant
description: **Job Description:** Join Our Team at Emerald Home Care as a Home Healthcare Assistant! Who are we? Emerald Home Care is a nurse led, Irish owner \- managed homecare company based in Blackrock, Dublin. We are not a part of a franchise or large corporation, we are entirely focused on providing high quality, local homecare in South Dublin. We provide a well\-run, ethical homecare service to our Clients and their families and we are an approved HSE Service Provider. Emerald is seeking compassionate, enthusiastic carers to work on an hourly basis to service our clients in the South County Dublin areas. Previous experience is ideal but not required, as we provide paid Caregiver Induction training, all you need is the right attitude and a passion for caring for others! * Location: Blackrock, Dun Laoghaire, Dalkey, Stillorgan, Dundrum, Churchtown, Crumlin, Drimnagh and surrounding areas. * Morning hours available (9am \-12pm availability). * Weekend hours available (morning and evening availability). * A driving licence and access to your own vehicle is preferred but our Home Care Assistants also use Bikes, E\-Bikes/Scooters, public transport and walk to client homes. What is Home Care? As a Home Care Assistant, you'll support people in their own homes, helping them stay independent and live with dignity in their community. Your workplace will change multiple times a day as you travel from home to home within a set local area. Your Role Will Include delivering high quality care and assistance to our clients such as: * Assisting with personal care (washing, dressing, toileting). * Supporting clients with mobility and transfers by Hoisting and utilising Patient Moving \& Handling practices. * Light meal preparation and feeding support. * Providing companionship and emotional reassurance while encouraging independence, dignity, and wellbeing. * Light housekeeping (tidying, laundry, etc.) * Prompting medication where necessary. * Accurately following and documenting care plans and reporting any changes or concerns in relation to clients directly to the office. Pay \& Benefits: * Starting Hourly rates from €15\.00 \- €20\.00 with premium pay on both Saturday and Sunday. * Double pay on Bank Holidays. * Bi\-weekly pay. * Mileage payment between client visits or Leap card subsidy payments. * Free vehicle breakdown cover during hours of work. * Flexible working hours to suit your availability. * Paid training which includes: QQI qualification, Induction Training and Online certified training courses. * 24/7 On\-Call Support. * Refer a friend bonus voucher. Employee Perks: * Free Patient Moving \& Manual Handling Training Provided. * Employee of the Month Awards. * No uniform required and all PPE supplied. * One to one support with initial shadowing shifts as you begin delivering homecare to our clients. * €100 joining bonus paid when you start work. * Employee Wellness Programme. What You'll Need: We welcome both new and experienced carers. * QQI Level 5 Healthcare Support Course or two core modules of Care Skills and Care of the Older Person are desirable but not necessary we pay for you to complete the core modules. * Possess a kind, positive, outgoing, friendly personality. * Willing to travel between clients: walking, using car, bike or public transport. * Competency in English \- both verbal and written. * Must live in the greater Dublin area with the right to work in Ireland. * Valid International Police Clearance and Visa (if applicable). * Full valid EU driving licence if you own your own vehicle. Ready to join our team? Apply directly to this advert! This is more than a job \- it's a chance to bring dignity, independence, and joy to those who need it most. Job Types: Full\-time, Part\-time Work Location: In person Job Types: Full\-time, Part\-time, Permanent Benefits: * Company pension * Employee assistance program * Free or subsidised travel * Sick pay * Wellness program Work Location: In person

title: Highways Project Engineer
description: **The Opportunity** We currently have a requirement for a Project Engineer to join our Roads team in Ireland. The successful candidate will work within the Transport Infrastructure team delivering a range of rural and urban design projects including major TII road projects, Minor TII road improvement projects, as well as urban street design incorporating Public Transport and Active Travel and Greenway projects. The successful candidate will gain invaluable experience within an established consultancy with flexible working options between home and office. They will be involved in a diverse and challenging range of projects for high profile clients within Ireland and further afield. This role also offers the successful candidate long\-term opportunities for career development. A role in the Highways team will include opportunities to input into design and coordination, often liaising with other multi\-disciplinary contributors to projects. You will be required to deliver projects to the required technical standards in a timely manner, reporting to the team leader or project manager as appropriate. You will have experience in planning and design of road schemes including feasibility studies, preliminary and detailed design, statutory processes, preparation of contract documents, contact administration and construction supervision. **As a Highways Project Engineer At Arup, You Will** * Prepare technical designs for projects in accordance with approved design standards and procedures in line with the delivery programme * Undertake research to inform updates to existing and the development of new national design standards and policy. * Ensure that relevant legislation, regulations, policies, strategies, and technical guidance is interpreted, followed and communicated across the team with guidance from senior members of staff * Implement the appropriate QA procedures for all deliverables and ensure compliance with H\&S requirements * Communicate effectively and in a team environment with a broad cross\-section of individuals. This would typically include but is not limited to; roads and streets team colleagues and project managers, other internal design disciplines, clients and contractors. **Does this role inspire you?** We're looking for someone who is passionate about Transport Infrastructure with a level 8 degree in Civil Engineering or similar. **We Also Value** * 3\-5 years post graduate experience, preferably in an Irish consultancy environment * Working towards chartered professional status * Technical Report writing * Working knowledge of Road Design Aspects (Geometric/ Drainage/ TSRM/ Pavement etc.) * AutoCAD * Excellent written and verbal communication skills and ability to work as part of a team * Ability to effectively manage your time and deliver projects in a timely manner **Desirable Experience** * Geometric Design experience (Open Roads Designer, Civil 3D or equivalent) * Drainage Design (MicroDrainage or similar) * TII Publications (Standards and Technical) * Traffic signs and road markings (Traffic Signs Manual) * Vehicle Restraint Systems * ArcMap GIS * Road Safety Audits **Why Arup?** At Arup, we believe in rewarding our members for their contributions to our shared success. As a trust\-owned company, we have the freedom to pursue meaningful work that aligns with our purpose and values. We also offer a unique profit share program, allowing our members to share in the results of our collective efforts. **Benefits That Work For You** * Professional Growth \& Development: Benefit from continuous learning opportunities, training programs, and mentorship to enhance your skills and advance your career at Arup. * Global Opportunities: Collaborate with colleagues worldwide and explore potential short\-term or long\-term assignments at other Arup offices and project locations. * Financial Well\-being \& Ownership: Receive a competitive salary and benefits package and share in Arup's success through our global profit\-sharing programme. * Wellbeing and Culture: At Arup we are committed to fostering a healthy working culture by offering a range of health and wellbeing benefits, including health insurance, flexible working schemes, wellness programmes and an Employee Assistance Programme. * Equality, Diversity and Inclusion: We strive to embed inclusion in our way of working, our interactions with one another and the projects we deliver. We are committed to creating an inclusive working environment for talented people of any background, based on merit, fairness and respect. At Arup, you belong to an extraordinary collective where individuality is encouraged. Our strength comes from how we respect, share, and connect our diverse experiences, perspectives, and ideas. You'll have the opportunity to do socially useful work that has meaning – to Arup, to your career, and to the clients and communities we serve. We are proud to be an equal opportunity employer and are committed to building a workplace that reflects the rich diversity of the world around us. We actively promote a culture of inclusion where everyone feels welcome, respected, and valued. We welcome applications from talented individuals of all backgrounds, regardless of age (within legal limits), gender identity or expression, disability, ethnicity, religion, sexual orientation, or any other protected characteristic. We are committed to making our recruitment process and workplaces accessible. **Do you want to join us in shaping a better world?**

title: IT & AV Support
description: Role Purpose The primary responsibilities of the IT Support \& AV Engineer will be to resolve end user issues efficiently and effectively, maintaining a friendly, approachable, and professional demeanour. As an IT Support \& AV Engineer, you will play a crucial role in ensuring the smooth operation of our Learning \& Development Centre, as well as the IT support function. This position is a blend of traditional IT and Learning Centre support, providing comprehensive technical assistance to our internal employees and external trainers. Team Summary Join a dynamic organisation where growth and positive change are constant, becoming a valued member of our collaborative IT team. In this dynamic environment, you'll have the opportunity to expand your skills and knowledge while contributing to the organisation's technical success. You will collaborate with and report to the Senior Support Engineer to align with team objectives and ensure effective IT service delivery. Main Duties and Responsibilities Desktop Support: * Respond to and resolve IT Service Desk tickets in a timely manner. * Provide local office support for hardware, software, and network issues. * Document and track issues using our ticketing system. * Troubleshoot and resolve desktop\-related issues, including performance problems and application errors. * Setup equipment including laptops and phones for new starters as well as managing the lifecycle of hardware. * Provide AV support to meeting rooms on site. * Ensure that asset management is kept up to date for the country located in for all assets. * Perform regular maintenance and updates to ensure optimal performance. * Support remote users with VPN and remote desktop solutions. **Learning Centre Support:** * Support visiting staff attending the training centre, resolving any IT issues they may have. * Proactively ensure internal and external trainers have IT needs met to ensure training sessions are smooth and trouble free. * Proactively manage and maintain training room audio and video facilities, working with 3rd parties where necessary. * Support any changes in needs for the training facility including events that may be hosted there. **Project Site Support:** * VisitProject Sites as needed to ensure smooth operation of onsite IT. * Resolve hardware, printing, networking and Wi\-Fi issues on site. * Assist where needed with the setup and decommission of sites. **General IT Support:** * Collaborate with other IT team members to implement and improve IT policies and procedures. * Provide training and guidance to employees on IT best practices and security protocols. * Participate in IT projects and initiatives as needed. * Provide support for company wide IT compliance certification. **Key Competencies** * Adaptability and initiative * Changing and improving * Managing a quality service * Energy and drive to deliver at pace **Desirable Skills and Experience** * At least 3\-5 years of experience in IT support, preferably in a Desktop Support role. * Experience in troubleshooting and resolving hardware, software, AV and network issues. * Experience with IT service management tools and ticketing systems. * Experience with Microsoft Teams and other Video Conferencing platforms. * Hands\-on experience in installing, configuring, and maintaining desktop hardware and software. * Experience in supporting remote users with VPN and remote desktop solutions. * Excellent people and customer service skills * Fluency in English (written and spoken) * Knowledge Base Documentation. * Ability to provide training and guidance to employees on IT best practices and security protocols.

You will report to your Team Manager who will aid in your professional development. **The Day\-To\-Day:** * Build long standing relationships with our clients, ensuring they always receive exceptional service * Manage a book of high\-net\-worth relationships with approximately ₠100\-150 million in assets * Be the conduit between our clients, our Investment Policy Committee, our research and trading departments, and several service teams * Proactively update clients on portfolio changes, financial markets, our current market strategy and investment philosophy * Gain advanced finance and capital markets education through our training program **Your Qualifications:** * 2\+ years exposure to private clients within the banking/wealth management industry * QFA or working towards QFA preferred * A sound knowledge of financial concepts, markets, current events and the market you will service your clients in * The ability to connect with and educate a variety of audiences * Able to maintain positivity and empathise with clients in good times and bad **Why Fisher Investments Ireland:** The global Fisher organisation distinguishes itself by putting clients first, providing unmatched service, and taking a personalised approach to investing. You can feel confident knowing that Fisher Investments and Fisher Investments Ireland align with our clients' priorities by using a simple and transparent fee structure and recognised European custodians. It's the people that make the Fisher purpose possible, and to help our employees meet their long\-term goals, we offer an array of benefits, including: * 100% paid premiums for our top\-tier supplemental healthcare and dental plans for employees and their qualified dependents * 28 days annual leave, with the ability to purchase up to 3 additional days per year, plus up to 11 paid holidays * Enhanced maternity pay package with 26 weeks' top up to full base pay for eligible employees * $10,000\* fertility, hormonal health and family\-forming benefit * A retirement pension plan, featuring a 9% company pension contribution of your base pay and an additional company match of up to 5% of base pay * Monthly gym subsidy of up to ₠50 per month * Employee Assistance Program and other emotional wellbeing services * A collaborative working environment that practices ongoing training, educational support and employee appreciation events * This is an in\-office role. Based on your role, tenure, and performance eligibility you may have the opportunity to participate in our hybrid work from home program. This program is subject to change. \*Employees residing outside of the US will be eligible for the $10,000 equivalent in their local currency. FISHER INVESTMENTS IRELAND IS AN EQUAL OPPORTUNITY EMPLOYER

In addition to the technical aspect of the role, the senior analyst also performs project work, looks for process improvements, partakes in training and development opportunities and is part of a larger team effort. **The Role** * + This role will enable a proactive loan closer looking to expand their experience within CLO operations and loan closing. The main goal of this position will be to support the CLO desk, utilising their passion to develop a career in operations. This role will provide great opportunity to learn and grow with very experienced colleagues. Below are key responsibilities: + Booking, confirming and settling loan trades for leveraged loan investments + Reviewing loan/credit documentation + Perform daily reconciliations of trade positions and cash + Identify operational risk and ensure all internal processes are accurately followed + Identifying and escalating issues, which could have significant impact on daily operations + Performing all tasks associated with the European Loan Operations team including (but not limited to) trade settlements, fail management, process reconciliation and accounting processing. + Providing exceptional service to the various business partners including portfolio managers, credit analysts, traders, counterparties and trustees. + Participating in future project opportunities and facilitate change in a rapidly developing environment. Experience with an Agile methodology an advantage but not essential. + Assisting with development and review for new and/or changes to departmental policies and procedures + Collaborating within team environment to successfully manage daily work volumes and quality standards **The Expertise And Skills You Bring** * + 2\-5 years of financial services industry experience. All of these combine to ensure that we are providing accurate and timely information into the NAV and then value to our investors. **Category** Investment Operations

title: Junior Investment Analyst

Fineco is one of the banks with the widest products range available. Fineco have developed a truly one\-stop solution which includes trading, investment and payment services. Fineco have developed a 100% proprietary platform for trading and investment which covers all customer needs, from trading listed and OTC products to investment products such as mutual funds and ETF. Fineco AM, which started its operations in May 2018, is authorized by the Central Bank of Ireland as a UCITS \& AIFM Management Company with a MIFID top up, and it currently has approximately €39bn in assets under management. Fineco AM is a competence centre\- specialised in the management of fund\-of\-funds, single strategy funds, as well as Structured Solutions Funds. Assist portfolio managers in the day\-to\-day operational management of fund\-of\-funds. * Prepare and maintain regular reporting, including performance, positioning, exposures, and internal outputs. investment managers. * Contributing to the review and analysis of due diligence questionnaires and supporting materials * Developing an understanding of different asset classes, investment strategies, and portfolio construction. **Key requirements:** * 0–2 years’ experience in financial services, internships, or relevant academic background * Educated to university level (Finance, Economics, or related discipline preferred) * Strong numerical and analytical skills * Good working knowledge of Excel * Interest in developing technical and analytical skills over time * Willingness to pursue professional qualifications (e.g. CFA) * Strong interest in financial markets and asset management * Highly conscientious with excellent attention to detail and accuracy * Strong work ethic and willingness to take ownership of tasks * Enthusiastic, proactive, and eager to learn * Organised and capable of managing multiple responsibilities effectively * A collaborative team player with strong communication skills **Desirable (not required):** * Exposure to financial market tools (e.g. Bloomberg, Morningstar, FactSet) * Awareness of emerging tools such as Copilot or data automation technologies **What we offer:** * **Growth Opportunities:** Unlock your potential with career growth and professional development paths. * **Innovative Team:** Be part of a dynamic and innovative team environment. * **Collaborative Culture:** Thrive in a collaborative company culture that values your contributions. * **Generous Time Off:** Enjoy 25 days of holidays to relax and recharge. * **Hybrid Working:** Benefit from a flexible hybrid working policy, 3 days in the office, 2 days flexibility to be remote. * **Health and Dental:** Comprehensive Healthcare and Dental coverage for your well\-being. * **Perks and Programs:** Access to TaxSaver, Bike to Work scheme, educational sponsorship opportunities, and professional memberships. * **Discretionary bonus:** Opportunity to earn a performance based discretionary bonus **Fineco Asset Management DAC is proud to be an Equal Opportunity Employer:** We are committed to enforcing non\-discrimination of candidates based on their religion, race, ethnicity, age, gender (including pregnancy and gender identity) disability, sexual orientation, civil or family status, or for being a member of the Traveller community. title: Junior Merchandiser
description: ### **About The Role** We are currently recruiting for a Junior Merchandiser to be based from our offices in Clonee. The role offers flexibility to work on a hybrid basis. The Junior Merchandiser will be responsible for supporting the coordination of outbound General Merchandise replenishment and inbound planning to maximise store availability and sales optimisation across Poundland \& Dealz stores. As Junior Merchandiser, you will: * Support Core and Promotional Outbound Replenishment alongside the Branch Merchandising Team * Ensure Core GM Availability targets are met in line with KPI * Manage Seasonal Outbound Replenishment * Manage stock allocation across multiple warehouses in UK and ROI to support efficient distribution to stores * Commercial and Merchandising Collaborative Planning ensuring contingency plans are agreed in advance to optimize store availability and minimize gaps during sell through periods * Identify and resolve stock\-related issues, ensuring minimal disruption to supply * Collaborate closely with the Merchandising team to align on inbound stock deliveries and priorities * Ensure Launch availability for Seasonal Events across distribution centres, avoiding overstocking and stock shortages * Monitor inbound inventory across distribution centres and regions, assessing availability by location and country, agitate and interrogate any gaps in availability or Range Plans * Manage the flow of stock into ROI, ensuring efficient allocation aligned with store demand * Execute inter\-depot transfers where required, optimising stock positioning rather than relying solely on direct UK\-to\-store distribution * Evaluate and select the most effective distribution routes, balancing DC picks and transfers to maximise efficiency We are looking for someone who will: * Be a technical specialist with operational knowledge of Supply chain (desired) * Have and understanding of how Supply Chain systems work (desired) * Have experience of working with Supply Chain replenishment systems (desired) * Have experience working in a fast\-paced supply chain, retail, or logistics environment. * Have great demonstrable collaborative working ethic. * Have ability to solve problems and make decisions, as well as to think * strategically and laterally * Be able to communicate and influence effectively at all business levels. * Have a key eye for detail and ability to analyse data. * Have a relevant qualification in business, supply chain, logistics and business change (desired) We offer rewards that go beyond the payslip, including: * 10% employee discount card with increased discount events over the year. * Celebrate your birthday, you’ll get an extra day off for your birthday. * 30 days holiday a year inc banks, rising by one day a year up to 33 days. * Annual bonus scheme, with the opportunity to earn up to 10% of your base salary. * We put wellbeing first through our partnership with Retail Trust which gives you and your family access to a fantastic range of resources. * My Staff Shop, giving you an amazing number of discounts and shopping vouchers. * For those unexpected moments, our partnership with stream enables you to access up to 50% of your monthly earned wages early, along with savings and financial advice. * Training and development opportunities, we want you to grow your career with us, be that through one of the qualifications we offer or internal programmes, this role can be just the first step. * Plus, life assurance, loyalty service awards and NAERSA pension. **Bring your energy, teamwork, and get stuck in approach — and we’ll help you build your career with us!** We’re committed and proud to our inclusive culture, and that starts with the recruitment and selection process. If you require a reasonable adjustment during the recruitment process, please do share this with us on your application form and we’ll make every effort to meet your needs.

What You’ll Be Working On You’ll be involved in: * Servicing and maintaining **HKC SecureWatch alarm systems** * Servicing and supporting **Hikvision CCTV systems** * Small upgrades and additions to existing alarm and CCTV systems * Fault finding and troubleshooting * Customer support and system demonstrations * Learning modern networking and IP\-based security technologies You’ll work across residential, commercial and specialist projects. If you are ambitious, technically minded, and want more than just “another job,” we’d like to hear from you. title: Junior Security Operations Engineer
description: **About Telnyx** *Telnyx is an industry leader that's not just imagining the future of global connectivity—we're building it. From architecting and amplifying the reach of a* *private, global, multi\-cloud IP network* *, to bringing* *hyperlocal edge* *technology right to your fingertips through intuitive APIs, we're shaping a new era of seamless interconnection between people, devices, and applications.* *We're driven by a desire to transform and modernize what's antiquated, automate the manual, and solve real\-world problems through innovative connectivity solutions. As a testament to our success, we're proud to stand as a financially stable and profitable company. Our robust profitability allows us not only to invest in pioneering technologies but also to foster an environment of continuous learning and growth for our team.* *Our collective vision is a world where borderless connectivity fuels limitless innovation. By joining us, you can be part of laying the foundations for this interconnected future. We're currently seeking passionate individuals who are excited about the opportunity to contribute to an industry\-shaping company while growing their own skills and careers.* **About The Role** We’re looking for a Junior Security Operations Engineer who is AI\-Forward to help scale and modernize our SecOps program. This is a hands\-on, builder role for someone who will design and ship the security tooling that powers our triage, investigations, and response workflows. You’ll report to the Technical Operations Director and work alongside our GRC lead to improve our vulnerability intake, threat response, darkweb posture, and internal security tooling. A core part of this role is building AI\-assisted security tooling: triage agents that pre\-classify bug bounty reports, investigation copilots that pull context from logs and SIEM data, response workflows that draft remediation steps and track them to closure. You’ll spend as much time wiring up that tooling as you will reproducing vulnerabilities and working incidents. This role suits someone who thrives in a lean, high\-impact environment, has strong opinions on where humans add value versus where tooling should take over, and wants to shape how a modern security team operates. **What You’ll Do** **Triage \& Vulnerability Management** * Review incoming vulnerability reports from our bug bounty intake; reproduce and document valid issues for engineering teams. * Build the tooling that improves signal\-to\-noise: automate duplicate detection, spam filtering, and abuse flagging so humans only touch what’s worth touching. * Act on DAST findings: coordinate remediation, verify fixes, and re\-test. * Track remediation timelines for critical vulnerabilities and keep stakeholders honest on SLAs. **Threat Response \& Monitoring** * Monitor and respond to EDR and cloud security alerts; investigate, contain, and document. * Analyze darkweb findings and credential exposures; shape our darkweb monitoring practices and tooling over time. * Improve detection coverage: tune noisy rules, close gaps, and enrich alerts with context so responders spend time on decisions, not data gathering. * Help configure and tune DLP, SIEM, and AI security tooling. **Security Tooling (core to this role)** * Build AI\-assisted triage tooling that pre\-classifies bug bounty reports, flags duplicates, and routes real issues to the right engineering team with reproduction context attached. * Build investigation tooling: LLM\-backed copilots and Slack bots that pull alert context, correlate signals across sources, and surface what actually matters. * Build response tooling: workflows that draft remediation steps, generate evidence artifacts, and track issues to closure across the teams that own them. * Evaluate emerging AI security tooling and bring what’s genuinely useful into the stack; retire what isn’t. * Apply a security\-minded lens to our own AI usage: prompt injection, data exfiltration, model misuse, and the guardrails that go around them. **Compliance \& Cross\-Functional** * Support audit evidence collection for SOC 2, ISO 27001, and PCI DSS. * Partner with ITOps to verify patches, endpoint coverage, and access hygiene. **You May Be a Good Fit If You Have** * Previous experience in a SecOps, Security Analyst, or Threat Response role. * Proven ability to understand and reproduce technical vulnerabilities. You can read a bug report and tell whether it’s real. * Experience with bug bounty triage (HackerOne, Bugcrowd, or similar). * Hands\-on exposure to SIEM, EDR, and DLP tools in production environments. * A genuine, demonstrated interest in applying AI/LLMs to security work: side projects, internal tooling, prompt engineering, agent frameworks, anything that shows you’ve actually built with this stuff. * Scripting and automation skills in Python, Bash, or similar. You reach for code before you reach for a checklist. * Comfort working autonomously across time zones and asynchronously. * Strong written communication. You can turn a messy investigation into a clear, defensible writeup. **Bonus Points For** * Experience building LLM agents, RAG pipelines, or Slack bots backed by OpenAI / Anthropic / open\-source models. * Detection engineering experience (Sigma rules, custom Falcon queries, Grafana/Loki dashboards). * Cloud security depth in AWS or GCP (IAM, workload posture, cloud\-native logging). * Prior work in regulated environments (SOC 2, ISO 27001, PCI DSS) from the practitioner side. * Exposure to AI/ML security concerns such as prompt injection, model evals, and data leakage through LLMs. * Public contributions: blog posts, CVEs, open\-source tools, CTF writeups. **Engagement \& Logistics** * Full\-time contract. * Remote\-first and async\-friendly. We have hubs in San Francisco, Denver, Dublin, and Amsterdam. If you’re near one, we’d love to see you in the office regularly; if you’re elsewhere in the world, that works too. * Potential to extend or convert based on fit. * Reports to the Technical Operations Director; works closely with the GRC lead and IT Operations. **How We Work** We’re a lean, high\-trust team. We value people who ship, who can operate independently, and who treat security as an engineering problem rather than a checklist. If you’re someone who sees a repetitive task and immediately thinks “this should be a script, or better yet, an agent,” you’ll fit in here. **To Apply** Tell us about a time you used AI, automation, or custom tooling to meaningfully change how a security workflow ran. What was manual before, what it looked like after, and what you learned. Links to code, writeups, or demos are welcome.

title: Kubernetes Engineer

Lots of focus around Kubernetes at scale, Infrastructure as Code, observability, automation and platform reliability across mission\-critical managed services and SaaS products. **Non\-Negotiables** * Kubernetes in large\-scale production environments * AWS, Azure and/or GCP platform engineering * Terraform and Infrastructure as Code * Helm * Strong Linux and containerisation experience * CI/CD and automation tooling * Kubernetes networking, security and observability * Experience working in complex enterprise environments * Ability to operate in highly available production systems * Senior\-level troubleshooting across cloud platforms and Kubernetes **What You’ll Work With** * Kubernetes * AWS / Azure / GCP * EKS / AKS / GKE * Terraform * Helm * GitOps workflows * ArgoCD * Prometheus * Grafana * Loki * Fluentd / Fluent Bit * RBAC * Secrets management * Multi\-cloud and hybrid infrastructure **Nice to Haves** * CKA / CKAD / CKS certifications * Service mesh technologies such as Istio, Linkerd or Cilium * Velero or Kasten * Spectro Cloud * Experience mentoring engineers or setting technical standards * Open\-source Kubernetes contributions * SRE practices and reliability engineering experience **Why Join** You’ll be joining during a major cloud transformation programme, helping build and scale Kubernetes platforms across AWS, Azure and GCP supporting critical global systems. There’s real ownership here too. Senior engineers will shape platform engineering standards and automation, while Principal\-level hires will influence architecture, cloud strategy and long\-term technical direction. The Dublin team is growing quickly, so it’s a good time to join if you want visibility, progression and the chance to help shape how the platform function evolves. **Senior to Principal Kubernetes Engineers \| Kubernetes, AWS, Azure \& GCP \| Multi\-Cloud Platform Build \| Global HealthTech**

title: Mechanical Engineer
description: **Job Title:** Mechanical Support Engineer (JuniorMid) **Location:** Dublin, Ireland **Type:** Contract (6\-9 months initially) **Sector:** Data Center Engineering / Mechanical Engineering ### **About the Role** Join Our Infrastructure Services as a junior\-to\-mid\-level Mechanical Support Engineer and play a key part in supporting global data centre cooling and airflow modelling. Working within the mechanical engineering team, youll collaborate with senior engineers. You will also work with stakeholders across design, construction, and sustainability. Together, you will enhance the efficiency and sustainability of global infrastructure. ### **Key Responsibilities** * **CFD Analysis:** Support computational fluid dynamics (CFD) analysis for airflow and cooling in data centres using tools such as HELYX, OpenFOAM, or similar. * **Simulation \& Reporting:** Post\-process simulation results and prepare clear, visual summaries for senior engineering teams. * **Model Validation:** Assist in validating CFD models against operational benchmarks and real\-world data. * **Collaboration:** Work with design and sustainability teams to assess cooling performance and water usage impacts. * **Tool Development:** Contribute to the development and improvement of in\-house airflow and water use modelling tools. * **Documentation:** Help document simulation assumptions, inputs, and interpretations for internal knowledge sharing. * **Learning \& Knowledge Sharing:** Engage in specific training and participate in team knowledge\-sharing initiatives. * **Innovation Support:** Occasionally assist with thermal/water modelling for experimental or emerging technologies. ### **Candidate Profile** #### **Basic Requirements** * Bachelors degree (or in final year) in Mechanical Engineering, Aerospace, or a related field. * Strong grasp of fluid dynamics and heat transfer principles. * Hands\-on experience with CFD tools (e.g., HELYX, OpenFOAM, ANSYS, SimScale, etc.). * Basic Python scripting and data analysis in Excel. * Excellent written and verbal communication skills. * Eagerness to learn specific design standards and CFD workflows. #### **Preferred Experience** * 13 years experience in HVAC, data centre cooling, electronics cooling, or building services. * Familiarity with psychometrics, airside economizers, or evaporative cooling systems. * Experience scripting or automating CFD workflows (Python, bash, macros). * Awareness of sustainability metrics (e.g., PUE, WUE). * Passion for sustainable, scalable, high\-performance infrastructure. ### **Whats on Offer** * Opportunity to work on global, large\-scale CFD projects in a leading\-edge environment. * Flexible, part\-time hours with full access to resources, tools, and mentorship. * Direct involvement in building and supporting world\-class infrastructure systems. * Career development and learning opportunities in computational engineering and sustainability. **To Apply:** If you have the technical skills, a passion for sustainability, and want to contribute to the future of data centre engineering, wed love to hear from you. Apply with your CV and a brief cover note outlining your relevant experience.

title: Mobile QA Architect
description: **About Us** Gen is a global company dedicated to powering Digital Freedom through its trusted consumer brands including Norton, Avast, LifeLock, MoneyLion and more. Our combined heritage is rooted in financial empowerment and cyber safety for the first digital generations, and today we deliver award\-winning cybersecurity, online privacy, identity protection and financial wellness solutions to nearly 500 million users in more than 150 countries. Together, we share a collective passion and vision to protect consumers and help them grow, manage and secure their digital and financial lives. We’re always looking for smart, fearless and high\-impact talent who see AI as a teammate – leveraging it to move faster and deliver meaningful results. When you’re part of Gen, you’ll have the flexibility, tools and support to do your best work and grow your career – from flexible working options and time off to competitive pay, benefits and well\-being programs. At Gen, we are scrappy and relentlessly customer driven. We create room for healthy debate, experimentation and continuous learning, and we seek out people with different experiences, identities and ideas to join our team. You’ll work with people who back each other, respect each other and understand that our differences are a competitive advantage. If this sounds like you, we’d love you to be part of Gen. **About The Role** We are pioneering an AI\-first development culture. Every aspect of our product development lifecycle is infused with AI adoption—from ideation and design to coding, testing, and deployment. Our mission is to lead the industry in AI\-powered mobile innovation. Quality assurance represents a critical leverage point for AI\-driven productivity in mobile engineering. In this role, you will help transform QA into a strategic, AI\-enabled function that drives both velocity and quality across the mobile engineering organization. **In This Role, You Will** * Build and mentor a world\-class mobile QA engineering team, ensuring high\-impact Android and iOS applications meet the highest quality standards for millions of users worldwide. * Champion AI\-first testing practices, leveraging tools such as GitHub Copilot, Claude Code, Cursor, and generative testing frameworks to automate test creation, optimize test coverage, accelerate bug detection, and elevate product quality. * Partner with engineering, product, and design teams to define and drive a comprehensive quality assurance strategy for mobile products. * Lead the evolution of test automation frameworks and infrastructure, leveraging AI to create intelligent, maintainable, and scalable test suites. * Foster a quality\-first mindset across the mobile engineering organization, positioning QA as a strategic partner rather than a bottleneck. * Remove blockers, set clear priorities, and enable your team to deliver high\-quality outcomes in a fast\-paced, global environment. **About You** * You are an inspiring leader who attracts and develops top talent, leads by example, communicates clearly, and builds trust through authenticity and competence * You have an AI\-native mindset and believe in AI’s transformative potential for quality assurance as a force multiplier, not a replacement * You bring deep technical expertise in mobile QA across Android and/or iOS, including understanding of device fragmentation, OS\-specific challenges, and robust test strategy design * You are a hands\-on manager who stays connected to the work, writes test code, and understands the day\-to\-day realities of your team * You have experience with programming languages such as Java, Kotlin, Swift, or Python * You are curious and continuously learning, especially in AI tooling for QA, mobile testing innovations, and team dynamics * You thrive in cross\-functional collaboration and build strong relationships with product, engineering, design, and DevOps teams * You are a strategic quality builder who shapes team culture, establishes meaningful quality metrics, and drives continuous improvement **What's Next** If you're excited about this opportunity, we’d love to hear from you! The hiring process consists of two technical interview rounds and one managerial interview. Throughout the process, you will have the opportunity to meet the hiring manager and future team members.

title: Network Engineer
description: Job Title: Network Engineer Company: Critical IT Infrastructure Managed Services Business Location: Dublin, Ireland Position Type: Full\-time, Hybrid Working We are working with a leading technology solutions provider based in Ireland, specializing in network infrastructure and IT services. We partner with organizations of all sizes to deliver innovative and reliable solutions that optimize their network performance, security, and scalability. As a Network Engineer you will be an integral part of our team, working on challenging projects and collaborating with talented professionals in a dynamic and supportive environment. ***Job Summary***:We are seeking a skilled Network Engineer to join our team and contribute to the design, implementation, and maintenance of our clients' network infrastructure. As a Network Engineer, you will be responsible for ensuring the stability, security, and performance of our clients' networks, as well as troubleshooting any issues that arise. You will work closely with cross\-functional teams and clients to understand their requirements, design scalable network solutions, and provide ongoing support. Responsibilities: * Design, implement, and maintain network infrastructure solutions for clients based on their specific requirements. * Configure and troubleshoot network equipment, including routers, switches, firewalls, and load balancers. * Perform network monitoring and analysis to ensure optimal performance and availability. * Identify and resolve network\-related issues, such as connectivity, latency, and security vulnerabilities. * Collaborate with cross\-functional teams to integrate network solutions with other IT systems and applications. * Develop and maintain network documentation, including diagrams, configurations, and procedures. * Conduct network capacity planning and make recommendations for network expansion and upgrades. * Stay up\-to\-date with industry trends, emerging technologies, and best practices in network engineering. * Provide technical support and guidance to junior network engineers as needed. Requirements: * Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Information Technology, or a related field (or equivalent work experience). * Proven experience as a Network Engineer or in a similar role. * Strong knowledge of network protocols, including TCP/IP, DNS, DHCP, VLANs, and routing protocols (e.g., BGP, OSPF). * Proficiency in configuring and troubleshooting network equipment from vendors such as Cisco, Juniper, or Palo Alto Networks. * Experience with network security technologies, including firewalls, VPNs, and intrusion detection/prevention systems. * Familiarity with network monitoring tools and techniques. * Ability to analyze and solve complex network issues efficiently. * Excellent communication and interpersonal skills, with the ability to work effectively in a team environment. * Strong organizational and time management abilities, with the capacity to handle multiple tasks and prioritize effectively. Preferred Qualifications: * Professional certifications such as CCNA, CCNP, JNCIA, or equivalent. * Experience with software\-defined networking (SDN) technologies. * Knowledge of cloud networking principles and technologies (e.g., AWS, Azure, Google Cloud). * Familiarity with network virtualization technologies (e.g., VMware NSX, Cisco ACI). * Experience with network automation and scripting (e.g., Python, Ansible). **How to Apply:**If you are a motivated Network Engineer with legal permission to work in Ireland and seeking an exciting opportunity to work with cutting\-edge technologies and make a significant impact in a collaborative environment, please submit your resume (CV) to the link provided for instant consideration.

title: Network Support Specialist - Sovereign Core
description: **Introduction** At IBM Software, we transform client challenges into solutions. Building the world’s leading AI\-powered, cloud\-native products that shape the future of business and society. Our legacy of innovation creates endless opportunities for IBMers to learn, grow, and make an impact on a global scale. Working in Software means joining a team fueled by curiosity and collaboration. You’ll work with diverse technologies, partners, and industries to design, develop, and deliver solutions that power digital transformation. With a culture that values innovation, growth, and continuous learning, IBM Software places you at the heart of IBM’s product and technology landscape. Here, you’ll have the tools and opportunities to advance your career while creating software that changes the world. **Role Overview** **Your role and responsibilities** IBM Sovereign Core is shaping the future of cloud and AI, for governments, public institutions and highly regulated enterprises by placing sovereignty, governance, and trust at the center of the platform. As a Senior Customer Support Engineer in IBM Sovereign Core, you will serve as a key technical specialist responsible for supporting highly secure, sovereign cloud environments. deployed across on‑premises, partner‑operated, and fully isolated (air‑gapped) infrastructures, built on Red Hat OpenShift and integrated with IBM’s AI, automation, and governance capabilities. You will handle complex incidents, provide deep troubleshooting expertise, and work closely with customers operating in highly regulated and jurisdiction‑sensitive environments. We are seeking a highly skilled Senior Network Support Specialist to help troubleshoot large‑scale, mission‑critical secure environments. This role focuses on advanced problem resolution, customer trust, and technical depth, with growing influence across teams and environments. You will work across modern data‑center architectures, cloud‑native platforms, and highly regulated infrastructures ensuring network services are resilient, robust, and high‑performing. Operate as a senior, independent resolver for complex issues, provide technical leadership in incident handling, and partnering effectively across teams. Due to the nature of this role and the associated client requirements, which include working with EU‑based customers in a sovereign environment, applicants must, by the job start date, lawfully reside in, be legally authorized to work in, and perform the role exclusively within the EU country of employment. **Preferred Education** Bachelor's Degree **Required Technical And Professional Expertise** * Strong hands\-on troubleshooting across TCP/IP, DNS, routing, and firewall/ACL controlled environments, with the ability to isolate fault domains quickly and accurately. * Deep operational knowledge of Red Hat OpenShift/Kubernetes networking, including pod/service networking, OpenShift Routes/Ingress Controllers, and NetworkPolicy\-driven segmentation. * Experience supporting restricted and disconnected (air\-gapped) environments, including internal registries/mirrors, proxy\-controlled egress, and tightly governed network change processes. * Support and troubleshoot BGP edge and internal routing issues * Diagnose and resolve control plane and data plane failures * Support multi\-tenant isolation using VLAN, VXLAN, and EVPN * Confident communicator who can work effectively with customer network/security teams and internal engineering/SRE to resolve complex incidents under strict sovereignty and compliance constraints. This role may require candidates to contribute to a shared shift and on‑call rotation as part of collaborative incident response support. **Preferred Technical And Professional Experience** **Preferred Additional Skills** * MetalLB and Load Balancer Operations Experience configuring and troubleshooting MetalLB , including VIP management, failover behaviour, and load‑balancer troubleshooting. * ACM Multi‑Cluster Networking Experience supporting hub\-spoke network topologies for managed clusters, including cross‑cluster connectivity and consistent network‑policy enforcement across multi‑cluster environments. * NetBox IPAM / DCIM Operations Hands‑on experience with NetBox data model and API usage, network device inventory management, and integration with network automation tools.

title: Oracle Flexcube Business Analyst (Contractor – 6 Months)
description: MUFG Investor Services is a trusted partner to many of the world’s largest public and private funds, providing asset servicing and operational solutions built for alternatives. With over $1 trillion in client assets under administration, we offer fund administration, banking, payments, fund financing, foreign exchange overlay, corporate and regulatory services, custody, business consulting, and more. Operating from 17 locations worldwide, we help clients mitigate risk, enhance efficiency, and navigate the operational complexities of today’s investment management landscape. As a division of Mitsubishi UFJ Financial Group (MUFG), one of the world’s largest financial institutions with approximately $3 trillion in assets, we combine deep expertise with the strength and stability of a leading financial institution. To learn more, visit us at www.mufg\-investorservices.com. **Job Description** As an Oracle Flexcube Business Analyst, you will play a critical role in helping MUFG Investor Services get more value from its core banking platform. We run Oracle Flexcube Universal Banking as the foundation of our banking operations – covering payments, treasury, foreign exchange, money markets, nostro reconciliation, SWIFT connectivity and more. The platform has grown significantly in capability and there is a real opportunity to close the gap between what Flexcube can do and what we currently use. You will work across IT and the business to identify standard features we have not yet activated, assess bespoke components built in earlier phases to determine whether they can be replaced by native functionality, and drive resolution of open configuration and change requests. You will work closely with the Banking Family Lead in IT and collaborate across Application Management, Banking Operations, and Product Management. You will also be a key point of contact with Oracle Support, coordinating on open tickets, change requests, and configuration queries – bringing clarity and momentum to engagements that have historically required significant back\-and\-forth. This is a hands\-on, delivery\-focused role that combines platform knowledge with strong stakeholder management and the ability to produce clear, well\-evidenced analysis and documentation. **Key Responsibilities** **Platform Capability Assessment** * Conduct a structured review of licensed Flexcube modules to identify features and configurations available but not currently activated or used. * Benchmark current usage across payments, standing instructions, foreign exchange, money markets, nostro reconciliation, and reporting against the full standard product capability. * Produce a prioritised inventory of unutilised capabilities with assessed business value and implementation complexity. **Bespoke Component Rationalisation** * Review customisations and bespoke components built in prior versions to determine whether equivalent standard functionality now exists in the current release. * Prepare structured assessments for each bespoke item: functional equivalence, migration complexity, risk, and recommended action (retire, retain, or replace). * Work with the Banking Family Lead and Application Management to validate findings and agree a rationalisation roadmap. **Backlog Management and Resolution** * Triage, document, and drive resolution of open items in the Flexcube Jira backlog, spanning configuration issues, change requests, business queries, and scripting requirements. * Distinguish clearly between items requiring Oracle involvement, items resolvable through internal configuration, and items needing formal change requests. * Maintain backlog hygiene: correct categorisation, clear ownership, and defined next steps for all items. **Oracle Engagement and Coordination** * Act as the primary coordination point for Oracle Support on open tickets and change requests, working alongside the Banking Family Lead and Application Management. * Prepare clear, well\-evidenced submissions to reduce round\-trip delays and accelerate resolution. * Track Oracle commitments, patch schedules, and responses, escalating where timelines slip. **Business and IT Collaboration** * Work with Product Management to translate business requirements into Flexcube configuration or change specifications. * Engage Banking Operations, Application Management, and project teams to gather requirements, validate solutions, and support UAT. * Produce high\-quality documentation: business requirements, functional specifications, solution designs, and training notes as required. **Reporting and Governance** * Provide regular progress updates to the Banking Family Lead on backlog status, capability findings, and Oracle engagement. * Flag risks and issues promptly, particularly where unresolved items represent operational or compliance exposure. **Qualifications** **You Have:** **Flexcube \& Core Banking Knowledge** * 5–8\+ years of hands\-on experience working with Oracle Flexcube Universal Banking as a business analyst, functional consultant, or implementation specialist. * Deep understanding of core banking operations including payments (domestic and cross\-border), SWIFT messaging, foreign exchange, money markets, and account management. * Experience engaging with Oracle Financial Services on support tickets, change requests, and product configuration – ideally including post\-upgrade optimisation work. * Ability to assess bespoke platform customisations objectively and build evidence\-based cases for rationalisation or replacement. **Business Analysis** * Proven ability to analyse and document complex functional requirements and translate them into clear specifications suitable for configuration or development. * Experience managing and triaging product backlogs, working across IT and business stakeholders to prioritise effectively. * Strong written communication skills; able to produce high\-quality documentation independently without extensive supervision. * Excellent stakeholder management – comfortable bridging technical IT teams and non\-technical business users simultaneously. **Technical Skills** * SQL proficiency – able to write and interpret queries to interrogate Flexcube data, validate title: Personal Trainer
description: **Company Description** With great earning potential and the opportunity to work within a fast paced, exciting and inspiring environment we are looking for a minimum of Level 3 qualified Personal Trainers (or CIMPSA Practioner certified) to work within our Everlast Gyms to continue to help our members find their ‘Greatness from within’. As an Everlast Personal Trainer you will work as part of a great team who help take our members from their ‘good’ to their ‘Great’. With a readymade client base, state of the art facility, best in class support and development, group training products and a rapidly growing brand, there is no better place to build your business and progress in your fitness career The Benefits As an Everlast Personal Trainer you will: * Keep 100% of your Personal Training Income * No yearly license fees * Have dedicated marketing to assist your business services * Gain additional member facing opportunities with our signature fitness products including Group Training classes * Showcase your services and skills as PT via our exclusive member onboarding program, MOVE * Access to our brand partner discounts and offers towards your education and future development * Take advantage of our CIMPSA academy partnership to gain yearly CPD’s * Receive full training from our internal Academy and development programs * Monthly catch ups and business mentorship from your Fitness Manager * Work for the UK’s fearless and most ground breaking fitness brand. Job Description As a Personal Trainer at Everlast Gyms, you will play a crucial role in helping our members achieve their fitness goals and discover their 'Greatness from within'. * Conduct one\-on\-one and small group training sessions, tailoring exercises to individual client needs and fitness levels * Develop personalised workout plans and nutritional advice for clients * Perform fitness assessments and track clients' progress * Demonstrate proper exercise techniques and ensure safe use of equipment * Motivate and encourage clients to push their limits and achieve their fitness goals * Provide guidance on injury prevention and recovery * Maintain a clean and safe workout environment * Participate in gym promotions and member retention initiatives * Stay updated on the latest fitness trends and techniques * Collaborate with other gym staff to create a positive and supportive atmosphere Qualifications * Level 3 Personal Trainer qualification (essential) * Strong knowledge of exercise physiology and nutrition * Excellent communication and interpersonal skills * Ability to create personalised workout plans tailored to individual client needs * First Aid and CPR certification * Experience in conducting fitness assessments * Passion for health and fitness * Motivational and enthusiastic attitude * Detail\-oriented approach to client progress tracking * Efficient time management skills * Organised and professional demeanour * Customer\-focused mindset with a commitment to client satisfaction * Willingness to continually learn and adapt to new fitness trends and techniques Additional Information Want to know see more of the great things our brand does please visit our LinkedIn page here. An opportunity like this at Frasers Group is for the fearless. The potential is massive, the experience unrivalled. To be able to make the most of it you need to live and breathe our principles: * Think without limits \- Think fast, think fearlessly and take the team with you * Own it and back yourself \- own the basics, own your role and own the results * Be relevant \- Relevant to our people, our partners and the planet
