title: Technology Lawyer
description: The market\-leading Technology and Innovation group in Matheson LLP (" **Matheson** ") is seeking to build on its ongoing growth and success. The group, which is the longest established technology practice in Ireland, is acknowledged to be at the cutting edge of technology related legal developments. The group advises on all aspects of technology related legal advice, providing strategic, practical and commercially focused advice to international and domestic clients across multiple sectors. The work includes advising on digital services and content regulation, cybersecurity, artificial intelligence, data protection and privacy matters, intellectual property and strategic and business critical contracts, both transactional and advisory. The group has grown substantially over the last several years and is recruiting ambitious senior associates who are keen to develop their expertise and help manage further continuing growth. **The role** This is a senior role within the Technology and Innovation group, where you will: * Lead on challenging and innovative technology law projects for a variety of clients; * Work collaboratively as part of multi\-disciplinary specialist teams (including Matheson’s Digital Economy Group) on high value and business critical client projects; * Manage junior and associate solicitors on client advisory matters across the full range of technology, digital services and privacy issues; * Participate in a variety of business development opportunities including foreign client relationship visits and representing Matheson at international conferences as well as the possibility of secondment to our London and Californian offices; * Help drive thought leadership in technology law through the Matheson client knowledge programme; and Advise on the legal issues across: * Large scale technology and commercial contract drafting and negotiations; * Data protection, data privacy and data governance; * Artificial intelligence; * Cyber security and operational resilience; * The digital single market including DMA and DSA compliance, content regulation for online platforms and intermediary industries, e\-commerce and online business practices for gatekeepers, platforms and businesses; * Transactional and advisory intellectual property projects. **The person** * You are an experienced Technology Lawyer with a strong track record both technically and in business development. * You are highly client focused and committed to consistently exceeding client expectations. * You have a strong academic track record and broad technical expertise. * You can manage a diverse workload, deliver quality, commercially\-focused advice with minimal supervision, and lead and develop junior lawyers. * You enjoy the law you practice, and are hardworking and resilient. You will thrive in a diverse and inclusive environment where you will work in partnership with top legal talent in a market leading team that is continuing to grow. * You will enjoy establishing relationships with a diverse range of clients and colleagues. * You are enthusiastic about business development, helping develop your junior colleagues to their maximum potential and how you can contribute to managing valuable client relationships as part of the team. **Why join Matheson** At Matheson you have the opportunity to work with exceptional people and a broad range of high calibre domestic and international clients. Working at Matheson provides you with an environment where you can thrive by living our values of Partnership, Respect, Innovation, Diversity and Entrepreneurship. Our Impactful Business Programme is delivered by a number of committees which drive key initiatives around D\&I, CSR, Environmental Sustainability and the Arts. We are also a founding signatory of the Pro Bono Pledge Ireland. We have a hybrid working policy. We provide an excellent working environment, a competitive benefits package and the opportunity to engage in a range of learning and development initiatives which are specifically tailored to your level. We are proud to support the Mindful Business Charter. *Accessibility: If you require any accommodation during the recruitment process, please contact us and we will work with you to ensure the best possible candidate experience.* *We value a diverse and inclusive workforce and are an equal opportunities employer.*

title: Test Analyst
description: We are seeking an experienced IT Test Analyst to join a dynamic technology team responsible for delivering high\-quality digital solutions. The successful candidate will play a key role in ensuring that new systems, enhancements, and integrations meet defined business and technical standards. This is a hands\-on testing role requiring strong analytical ability, a structured approach to quality assurance, and a commitment to excellence. **Role Purpose** The IT Test Analyst will be responsible for planning, executing, and coordinating all phases of software testing across multiple projects. This includes developing test strategies, designing test cases, managing test environments, and collaborating closely with development and business teams to ensure successful delivery. **Core Responsibilities** * Analyse business and technical requirements to understand system functionality and testing needs. * Design and maintain comprehensive test plans, scripts, and scenarios to validate functionality, usability, performance, and security. * Conduct manual and automated testing activities to verify system behaviour and data integrity. * Manage test environments, datasets, and configurations to support accurate and repeatable test execution. * Identify, log, and track software defects through to resolution using appropriate tools and processes. * Provide clear, concise reporting on test outcomes, risks, and quality metrics to stakeholders. * Collaborate closely with developers, analysts, and project managers to resolve issues and enhance solution quality. * Support the continuous improvement of testing processes, tools, and documentation standards. **Experience \& Qualifications** * Minimum 3 years’ experience in a software testing or QA role within a technology\-focused environment. * Relevant third\-level qualification in Information Technology, Computer Science, or a related field. * Proven experience testing web, cloud, or enterprise applications. * Strong knowledge of software testing principles, tools, and methodologies. * Understanding of the software development lifecycle (SDLC) and common delivery approaches such as Agile or Waterfall. * Experience with defect management and test management tools (e.g., JIRA, Azure DevOps, TestRail). * Ability to create and maintain traceability between requirements, test cases, and results. * Excellent analytical, documentation, and communication skills. * Strong attention to detail and a commitment to producing high\-quality work. * Ability to work independently and contribute effectively within a team setting. **Desirable Attributes** * Professional certifications in software testing or quality assurance (e.g., ISTQB Foundation Level or equivalent). * Experience with test automation frameworks or scripting languages. * Exposure to API, integration, or performance testing. * Familiarity with DevOps and CI/CD environments. * Demonstrated initiative, curiosity, and a proactive approach to learning new tools and technologies. **Personal Qualities** * A structured thinker who enjoys problem\-solving and continuous improvement. * Excellent communicator who can translate technical concepts for non\-technical stakeholders. * Reliable, accountable, and focused on delivering results. * Adaptable and comfortable working in a fast\-paced environment with changing priorities.

title: Test Engineer
description: Job: Test Engineer Location: Sandyford, South Dublin Salary: 40,000 / 200 per day Working type: On Site 5 days a week Type: 12 month\+ contract We are looking for a talented Test Automation Engineer to join our banking customer's dynamic team, responsible for ensuring the quality and reliability of their customers banking network. This role involves manually testing the likes of ATM machines, assessing the ATM Network and checking for software upgrades. You will move the test process to writing automated tests and managing the automation testing process. This role is for the more ambitious as the business are keen to take on someone eager to progress their career, learn and grow in the role to assume more responsibility. We are looking for people with the below skillset \& experience: * Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Information Technology or a related field * Experience with software testing, preferably in the banking or financial services industry * Familiarity with automated testing tools and frameworks, such as Selenium, FIS or Paragon software. * Competent with Java scripting * Strong analytical and problem\-solving skills, with a focus on quality and attention to detail * Excellent communication and interpersonal skills, with the ability to work effectively in a team environment * Reliable, self\-motivated and willing to work in a physically demanding environment * Ideally some knowledge of software development methodologies, such as Agile or Scrum Some of the key responsibilities include: * Test ATMs for software upgrades, ensuring compatibility and functionality across various models and configurations * Develop and execute automated test scripts to validate ATM software performance and functionality * Collaborate with the development team to identify and report defects, and work together to resolve issues * Manage the automation testing process, including creating and maintaining automated test suites, and monitoring test results * Stay up\-to\-date with the latest testing tools and techniques, and recommend solutions to improve the testing process * Contribute to the development of test plans, test cases, and test scripts * Participate in code reviews and provide feedback on testing\-related issues * Ensure that all testing activities are conducted in accordance with the bank's policies and procedures This is a really exciting role that will see excellent career progression in a supportive environment. If you would like to know more about the role, please submit your CV to the link provided for immediate consideration.

title: Textiles Merchandiser
description: Dunnes Stores is Ireland’s leading retailer positioned at the cutting edge of food, fashion and home retailing. We have over 130 stores in Ireland, Northern Ireland and Spain, a strong e commerce business and are expanding our service and product offering all the time. The Textiles Merchandise Controller key function is to create and manage the Open to Buy and stock targets for the season. They are responsible for maintaining the availability of core \& volumes lines and developing the overall range plan with the buyer agreeing quantities, ranging by grade and the intakes required to achieve the budget, reforecasting in season and the actions on aged stock / slow sellers and markdowns. **Responsibilities:** **Planning:** * Planning seasonal Budgets at section class level with the Department Buyers * Set the related Open to Buy plan for the season, update and coordinate the actions on potential overtrades/ underperforming areas with the buying team to adjust the forward buy requirement * Sign off of the core \& volume lines units within the weekly forecast sheets and ensuring continuous flow from suppliers * Purchase order sign off – ensure intakes are within the Dept OTB by week and are planned within the sales plan allowing for a launch allocations and replen for all stores ranged. Confirming the charges, pricing and margin are accurate and within the budget plan. * Co\-ordinate buy volumes with Online / Regions within the budget /open to buy. * Working with buying team and Online Merchandising team to maintain online availability, agreeing season \& launch qtys, Online specific packs and ensuring replenishment is actioned daily. * Manage launches Fashion \& Transitional stories and seasonal events. * Planning and controlling Department overheads. **Stock Management:** * Identify current shortages / oversupply of stock levels within stores/ online / warehouse and agree the actions to be taken. * General markdowns (slow sellers / seasonal clean ups etc.) – Identify and action. * Reviewing size ratio issues and confirming changes on forward cuts with suppliers. * Monitoring store performance for sales, intake, stockholding, markdowns etc. through regular contact with stores and store visits * Margin checks receipt v booked to adjust correct charges on Purchase orders. * Manage shipping approvals within the open to buy; including working with suppliers on shipment plan / late deliveries / cancellations etc. * Follow up with Shipping Team on container status, paperwork and inbound issues. * Manage bulk returns / supplier penalties daily. * Supplier analysis \& compliance. * Work with the Spanish team on the container fills and keep the auto replenishment upload updated for the correct minimum fills and related SOM exclusion files. **Admin** * Overseeing the administrative function of the buying departments and developing the skills of the Trainee Merchandiser and Administrator including general allocations to stores / online **If this sounds like the job for you, please apply now!** **Dunnes Stores is an Equal Opportunities Employer**

title: Tourism & Operations Internship
description: **ABOUT US** Indie Campers, the go\-to campervan Marketplace, is on a mission of making road trips available to everyone. Thanks to its digital approach and customer\-centric orientation, Indie Campers has developed a strong booking experience and high\-quality road trips at affordable prices. With over one million nights rented through our travel platform, we have welcomed more than 300,000 travelers from 169 countries. We offer a comprehensive and expanding array of road trip possibilities: short\-term RV rentals, long\-term campervan subscriptions, and the possibility of buying one of our vehicles available for sale. Rooted in a deep technological focus, our challenges are both exciting and demanding and require top talent and motivation to be successfully pursued. We are growing our team and looking for those interested in pursuing this dream with us and joining an ever\-developing journey. **THE ROLE** We're on the lookout for passionate students or recent graduates who want to dive into the exciting world of RV and campervan rentals and gain hands\-on tourism experience at our **Dublin depot** at **Clonswords Yard Ballyboughal Co Dublin A41F308, Ireland.** As our **Tourism \& Operations Intern**, you'll be the friendly face of our brand \- welcoming guests, setting them up for road trips of a lifetime, and ensuring their journey ends just as wonderfully as it began. If you thrive on meeting new people, love a challenge, and enjoy the buzz of a fast\-paced environment, your next adventure starts here! **WHAT WILL YOU WORK ON?** **Front\-office activities:** * **Customer service and sales:** Take care of check\-ins and check\-outs, register guests, process payments, explain campervan functionalities, terms and conditions, upsell road trip extras. **Back\-office activities:** * **Operations handling:** Align the reservations with the available campervans, verify and prepare campers and road trip extras and kits. * **Quality control:** Maintain the campervans in top condition, identify and act on possible maintenance or repair needs. * **Support activities:** Support the team in monitoring the existing stock, coordinating the fleet of campervans, troubleshooting operational issues. **WHO ARE WE LOOKING FOR?** * You are a student or recent graduate in Hospitality, Tourism, International Studies, Business Administration or a related field looking for an internship; * You are fluent in English, have a work permit (if applicable), and avalid driver's licence (for both manual and automatic vehicles); * You like international environments and meeting new cultures; * You possess a natural talent for connecting with people and have a strong customer service orientation; * You have a problem\-solving mindset, thriving to create solutions and feeling comfortable with getting your hands dirty. **THE INDIE COMMITMENT** * All internships are compensated; * Online and on\-the\-job training to develop relevant skills to your growth: guest relations, customer service, problem\-solving, communication, team spirit, organization, time management, fleet coordination, supplies management and logistics; * International opportunities and possibility of integration in Indie Campers after the internship. ***Are you ready to Go Indie?***

description: **Peter Mark \- Pavilions, Swords** We want you to come and train with us if you aspire to be the best, have style and enjoy being creative. **If you are interested in working with us please read on‿‿‿** **Why work for us?** * Peter Mark’s long standing belief is to encourage and develop talent * You will also attend our Training Schools to complete training courses in line with our exceptional Peter Mark Career Path which covers every aspect of hairdressing * On completion of the training programme you will be a qualified Stylist **Peter Mark offer you plenty of benefits to build your career including:** * Competitive salary * Exceptional training \& development * Career progression opportunities (management \& senior roles) * Group Pension Scheme * Group Scheme Discounts * Bike to Work Scheme * Travel Tax Saver Scheme * Employee Assistance Programme * Management Training Programme * Company social events \& lots of fun along the way! **If you want to become a Trainee Hairstylist you must have:** Customer service experience An excellent level of English **Peter Mark are equal opportunity employers** Job Types: Full\-time, Fixed term Work Location: In person

title: Trainee Industrial Painter – Manufacturing
description: **Trainee Industrial Painter \- Manufacturing** We are recruiting a trainee painter to join our paint shop team. This role is ideal for someone practical, physically fit and interested in learning a skilled trade in manufacturing. No previous experience is required. Attitude and willingness to learn are more important than experience. You will be trained in industrial paint systems used in heavy manufacturing, working alongside experienced team members and with support from a world\-class paint supplier. This is not a quick course. Learning to paint properly takes time. Over approximately three years you will gain solid industrial painting experience, a valuable skill used across engineering, transport and manufacturing industries. This role suits someone who enjoys practical work and wants to learn a skilled trade rather than work in an office. **What The Job Involves** Preparing steel equipment for painting, including time working in the blast shop Assisting with spray painting and finishing Learning industrial coating systems and application techniques Maintaining high quality standards in the paint shop **What We Are Looking For** Physically fit and comfortable with hands\-on manual work A good attitude and willingness to learn Reliable and punctual Interested in developing a practical trade **What We Offer** Structured on\-the\-job training Support from experienced team members Training with a leading international paint supplier The opportunity to build a long\-term skill in manufacturing **Salary** Starting rate from €15\.00 per hour during the initial training period Pay increases on a sliding scale as skills and experience develop Fully trained industrial painters typically earn €18 \- €22 per hour depending on experience Portnahinch, Portarlington, R32CD40 Monday to Friday 8\.30\-5\.00

title: Trainee Optical Assistant
description: **Specsavers, Citywest** Trainee Optical Assistant So, you’re a proactive people person, passionate about retail and offering the best customer service? Sounds like you’d be a great fit here. So, if you’ve ever considered a career in optics, this Trainee Optical Assistant role could be perfect for you. As a Trainee Optical Assistant, you don’t need to have previous experience, you just need to be prepared to create an outstanding store environment for both our customers and our team, making sure every patient receives the best care in our stores. We aren’t looking for people with years of experience and heaps of qualifications, just passionate, hard\-workers ready to confidently welcome and help customers as they walk through our doors. And once you’ve found your feet as a Trainee Optical Assistant, you don’t just have to stop there. We have plenty of professional training and development in other business areas, as well as the support of experienced Opticians and Technicians supporting you on your journey. So, whatever you want your career to look like, you can bring it to life at Specsavers. **Our store** Based in Citywest Shopping Centre we are a community driven store that actively participates with charities and community initiatives alike. **What’s on Offer?** As well as all the support you need and great training and development opportunities, we have a whole load of benefits on offer for you to enjoy. These include: * Full Time Hours \- 37\.5 \- weekends included * Bonus and Staff Discounts * Outstanding clinical and professional development opportunities * Access to latest clinical technology and equipment **What we’re looking for?** If this is still sounding like a bit of you, we do need you to have a few skills to get started as a Trainee Optical Assistant. These include: * Passion for providing excellent customer service and making customers’ lives easier * Great teamwork * A good initiative * Confidence in using IT systems * Excellent communication and listening skills * Prepared to step out of your comfort zone and try something new * Able to receive feedback and be open to development, knowing we’ll be there to support and guide you throughout your process (with your openness and our guidance, your opportunities will be endless). **Find out more** If you’ve got all these skills, we’d love for you to apply, but there’s also some desirable experience that could set you apart from the competition if you have it. This includes * Previous experience in a fast\-paced customer service environment * Experience in optics or Audiology business * Basic knowledge/experience of optical and/or Audiology terminology. Checked all the boxes? Now’s the perfect time to apply!

title: Training Specialist
description: **DON’T BE IN THE DARK ABOUT YOUR CAREER…….** **WE WANT YOU TO HELP US CREATE THE DIGITAL FUTURE OF OUR COMPANY** We are looking for exceptional people to join our diverse and multi\-cultural team. This is a fantastic opportunity to play a lead role as we connect process, data and technology, creating sustainable business benefits and drive the achievement of Divisional, Sales Area and Common Function business outcomes. As we are creating a new operating model, this presents a great opportunity for learning and career development in this full\-time position. **We are now recruiting for a Training Specialist – (Hybrid), a permanent role, based in Dublin, Ireland.** This is a role reporting to the Head of Change Management and Training. The organization seeks to drive performance excellence through process improvement and delivering on desired business outcomes across all E2E processes. **JOB PURPOSE** The Training Specialist is a key role within the Change Management and Training team, supporting global process, system, AI, and digital transformation initiatives through role\-based learning and enablement activities. Responsibilities include supporting the design, development, coordination, and continuous improvement of learning solutions aligned to approved business processes and ways of working. Working closely with the Training Lead, Change Management Lead and process teams, the Training Specialist helps employees build confidence in new processes, systems, and ways of working to support operational readiness and successful adoption. This role contributes to business adoption through practical, learner\-focused enablement activities and learning support across transformation and continuous improvement initiatives. **Main Responsibilities** * **Design and develop enablement materials:** Create role\-based learning and support materials including e\-learning, job aids, workflow guides, facilitator guides, and learner support materials based on approved business processes and aligned to agreed learning standards and governance requirements. * **Plan and coordinate learning interventions:** Coordinate training schedules, logistics, communications, and learner administration for virtual and instructor\-led sessions. Support instructors and delivery teams to ensure learning activities run smoothly across global audiences. * **Support training needs analysis and planning:** Work with process teams and the Training Lead to support the identification of role\-based learning needs and recommended enablement approaches. * **Support super\-user enablement activities:** Assist with onboarding materials, communications, and learning support activities for super\-users and local business support networks. * **Contribute to readiness and adoption activities:** Support learning readiness activities, track training and adoption metrics, and help identify improvement actions where required. * **Collaborate across process and program teams:** Partner with process teams and subject matter experts to develop practical learning materials and support a consistent user adoption approach. * Other Ad hoc Duties as requested by management. **Experience** * Experience supporting learning and enablement activities within transformation or technology implementation environments. * Experience developing digital learning, enablement, or communication materials. * Strong interpersonal and stakeholder coordination skills across global teams. * Ability to create visually clear and user\-friendly learning and communication materials. * Experience supporting training needs analysis and role\-based learning planning. * Organized and structured approach to managing priorities and stakeholder relationships. * Comfortable working across cultures, time zones, and fast\-paced delivery environments. Essential: * Minimum 3 years’ experience supporting learning or training activities within transformation or technology\-enabled environments. * Understanding of instructional design principles and learner\-focused content development. * Experience developing learner\-focused training and enablement materials. * Experience supporting training needs analysis and role\-based learning planning. * Strong written communication skills with the ability to produce clear and audience\-appropriate materials. Desirable: * Exposure to instructional design methodologies or certifications (e.g. ADDIE, SAM, ATD, CIPD, or equivalent). * Experience working within an ERP or enterprise systems transformation environment (SAP, Oracle, or equivalent). * Familiarity with simulation or digital adoption tools (e.g. SAP Enable Now, ClickLearn, WalkMe, or equivalent). * Experience deploying SCORM\-compliant learning content to an LMS (e.g. Workday Learning, Cornerstone, or equivalent). * Experience using data analysis and reporting tools such as Excel or Power BI to support learning insights and adoption reporting. * Experience using visual communication or content creation tools (e.g. Canva, Adobe Express, PowerPoint, or similar). Other: * Occasional travel may be required. **What we offer:** * 22 days annual leave * 4 Company Days * Defined Contribution Pension * Onsite gym * VHI Health Insurance * Life \& Disability Insurance * Health \& Wellbeing Initiatives – onsite massage therapist, gym membership, wellbeing seminars, bike to work scheme, etc * Flexible start \& Finish times * Hybrid working **Location**: Dublin, Ireland (Dockline, Mayor Street, IFSC, Dublin 1\) **Reporting to**: Head of Training and Change Management. **Diversity, Inclusion \& Sustainability** Sandvik has a developed belief in conducting our business in a sustainable and responsible manner, both for our employees and the environment; wherever in the world we operate. Our high ethical standards determine our policy of putting Safety at a premium, acknowledging and respecting the communities and giving due consideration to the environment in the areas we operate. These core values, to prioritise the safety and well\-being of our employees, are the foundation of how we work today. We also recognise the importance on inter\-personal relationships and how those based on honesty, respect and trust, enhance our working lives and help each other’s growth and career development. Equality of opportunity is fully supported and endorsed as an employer by Sandvik, while also welcoming the additional strength that diversity brings, by providing a work environment where everyone is included, treated fairly and with respect. **Sandvik Mining and Rock Solutions** Mining is a business area within the Sandvik Group and a global leading supplier of equipment and tools, parts, service, digital solutions and sustainability\-driving technologies for the mining and construction industries. Application areas include rock drilling, rock cutting, loading and hauling, tunneling and quarrying. In 2024, sales were approximately 64 billion SEK with about 17,000 employees. **A glimpse of our culture** At Sandvik, we’re tech driven, innovative and entrepreneurial! For us, success is a team effort \- we value diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive culture where people can be themselves and reach their full potential. So, we invest in supporting each other, learning together and celebrating our differences. We also give you the freedom to find that perfect mix between work, family life and interests. Visit our stories hub, LinkedIn or Facebook to get to know us further. **How to apply**? Interested in the challenge? Please apply via this advert or directly at https://www.home.sandvik/career Prior to this recruitment, we have already decided which advertising channels and marketing campaigns we wish to utilise, and we respectfully decline any contact from marketing or recruitment agencies regarding additional channels or campaigns. **The closing date for applications is** **25th May 2026** **Applicants must be eligible to work in the EU or hold a valid permit to work in the EU**

title: Transformation Change Analyst - Customer Availability
description: Because we strive to put people first. Culture, our way. **Technology at Primark** What’s a career at Primark all about? The positive impact you’re making, the experiences you’re having and the people you’re with. You’re our inspiration. Embrace what sets you apart, own your career and develop in ways you never expected. Leave your mark. And do it your way. Our technology team is actively shaping the next wave of advancements. Engaged with innovative initiatives, your expertise will propel our business into the future. Collaborating with a creative team of tech enthusiasts, you’ll contribute your unique skills to fuel our technological advancements. **What You’ll Do as** **Transformation Change Analyst \- Customer Availability** In your role, you’ll collaborate with different people across a range of skillsets. Here’s a flavour of your day\-to\-day: * Support delivery of change initiatives at project/workstream level, working across multiple projects within the same functional area, contributing to impact analysis, stakeholder engagement, communications, training, and readiness activities * Conduct change impact assessments, stakeholder mapping, and data gathering, while supporting the preparation of communications, training content, and engagement materials to guide colleagues through change * Coordinate stakeholder sessions, workshops, and feedback loops, ensuring effective engagement and capturing insights on risks, resistance, and adoption themes * Assist with readiness assessments and adoption tracking, providing meaningful insights into progress, risks, and overall organisational readiness * Support execution of project\-level change activities and timelines, including go\-lives, hypercare, and surveys, while maintaining accurate documentation, reporting, and tracking * Collaborate closely with Change Managers/Leads, SMEs, and impacted teams to deliver a consistent experience, build strong relationships, and escalate risks, resistance, or key insights promptly. **What You’ll Get** This role is a hybrid opportunity, offering 2\-3 days Working from office. People are at the heart of what we do here, so it’s essential we provide you with the right environment to perform at your very best. Let’s talk lifestyle: Healthcare, pension, and potential bonus. 27 days of leave, plus bank holidays and if you want, you can buy 5 more. Because Primark is all about tailoring to you, we offer Tax Saver Tickets, fitness centre, and a subsidised cafeteria. **What You’ll Bring** Here at Primark, we want everyone to feel valued – so please bring your authentic self to work, of course with some other key experience and abilities for this role in particular: * Degree (or equivalent experience) with 1–3\+ years’ experience in Change, Transformation, PMO, Communications, HR, or related roles, including supporting project or change delivery activities * Familiarity with core change management concepts (impacts, stakeholders, training, communications) and experience preparing materials, documentation, and internal reports * Exposure (nice to have) to large transformations, technology deployments, or operational change, ideally within retail, FMCG, or other fast\-paced environments, along with experience in survey tools, data gathering, or basic analytics and an interest in Change Management (certification optional) * Strong organisation, coordination, and written communication skills, with the ability to create clear materials, interpret impacts, risks, and feedback, and maintain strong attention to detail * Ability to use data to generate insights (Excel, dashboards, survey tools), work with collaboration tools (Teams, Miro, etc.), build positive stakeholder relationships, and support reporting, workshop facilitation, and basic design (nice to have) * Curious, proactive, and eager\-to\-learn team player who is comfortable working in ambiguous, fast\-changing environments, and is energetic, reliable, and delivery\-focused Does this sound like you? Great, because we can’t wait to see what you’ll bring. You’ll be supported within a team of equally capable people, celebrating who you are and aiding you reach your potential. At Primark, we’re excited about our future \- and we’re excited to develop yours. **About Primark** At Primark, people matter. They’re the beating heart of our business and the reason we’ve grown from our first store in Dublin in 1969 to a £9bn\+ turnover business and over 80,000 colleagues and over 440 stores in 17 countries today. Our values run through everything we do. In essence, we're Caring and always strive to put people first. We're also Dynamic, bravely pushing the boundaries to stay ahead. And finally, we succeed Together. If you need any reasonable adjustments or have an accessibility request, during your recruitment journey, such as extended time or breaks between online assessments, a sign language interpreter, mobility access, or assistive technology please contact your talent acquisition specialist. All offers of employment are subject to background checks, including right to work, reference education and for some roles criminal, and financial checks. If you have any concerns, please reach out to our talent acquisition team to discuss. **REQ ID:** JR\-8881

title: UI Engineer
description: We’re partnering with a well\-funded early\-stage startup building an AI\-powered operational intelligence platform for the food manufacturing industry. The team is tackling complex forecasting, production planning, and supply chain optimization problems through advanced predictive analytics and modern AI systems. They’re now hiring a Founding Design Engineer to own both product experience and frontend development for a highly interactive B2B platform. What You’ll Do * Lead end\-to\-end UX and frontend development * Build intuitive interfaces for complex forecasting and operational data * Design AI\-native workflows and chatbot experiences * Create interactive dashboards and data visualizations * Collaborate closely with product, research, and engineering teams * Drive rapid iteration using product analytics and user feedback Requirements * Strong experience designing B2B SaaS products * Expert in Figma, React, Next.js, TypeScript, and TailwindCSS * Experience visualizing complex datasets and time\-series data * Familiarity with AI/LLM products and streaming AI interfaces * Startup mindset with strong ownership and execution skills Additional Info * Competitive salary \+ meaningful equity * 35 days paid leave \+ strong benefits package * No visa sponsorship available * Must be authorized to work in Ireland

title: Underwriting Operations Assistant
description: **Master a Specialized Financial Field inside a Growing MGA** The specialized insurance market is evolving rapidly, and the most successful businesses are those built on clean data and absolute operational precision. We are representing a rapidly growing Managing General Agent (MGA) based in Dublin 2 that is expanding its operations team due to a significant surge in market volume. This isn't a static administrative position; it is an entry point into a highly specialized financial niche where an inquisitive mind can thrive, learn, and master complex market mechanisms. At Morgan McKinley, we partner with employers who understand that technical operations form the bedrock of underwriting profitability. This business has built an exceptional market reputation by combining a structured, professional framework with an expanding footprint that offers true long\-term development. **The Impact: What You Will Achieve** Instead of executing repetitive tasks without context, you will be given the autonomy to learn how a scaling MGA operates from the inside out. You will own the technical workflow that allows underwriters to make accurate decisions, serving as a critical operational link across multiple business areas. Your daily focus will centre on: * **Submission Management:** Prepare comprehensive insurance submissions by inputting key data into internal systems and extracting insights from specialized information services. * **Binding \& Execution:** Manage the technical binding process for key product lines, ensuring full alignment with internal risk management systems. * **Documentation Control:** Draft precise policy documentation under the guidance of the management team while protecting the total integrity of database records. * **Cross\-Department Support:** Provide essential data entry assistance to the actuarial team and maintain accurate records within the broker contact system. - **Foundations for Success** We want to connect with professionals who love to look beneath the surface and figure out how things work. To fit into this growing operation, you should bring: * **Background:** Prior experience within the insurance sector gives you a distinct advantage, but a solid foundation in a structured commercial business environment is essential. * **System Literacy:** Robust familiarity with computer database systems alongside day\-to\-day proficiency in Microsoft Word, Excel, and PowerPoint. * **Precision:** An innate capability to organize and prioritize your own workload with a sharp attention to detail. * **Communication:** Clear written and verbal communication skills to coordinate effectively with underwriters and broader company departments. **The Perks** * **Location:** Central, modern office space situated in Dublin 2\. * **Financial Security:** Highly competitive base salary alongside a generous pension scheme. * **Commuter Benefits:** Travel cards provided to fully cover your daily commute. * **Growth:** A genuine opportunity for an inquisitive mind to build deep expertise in an expanding financial sector. If you're interesting in finding out more, apply now or reach out to Hamish Ross at Morgan McKinley.

title: User Experience Researcher (Bangkok-based, Relocation provided)
description: **About Agoda** At Agoda, we bridge the world through travel. Our story began in 2005, when two lifelong friends and entrepreneurs, driven by their passion for travel, launched Agoda to make it easier for everyone to explore the world. Today, we are part of Booking Holdings \[NASDAQ: BKNG], with a diverse team of over 7,000 people from 90 countries, working together in offices around the globe. Every day, we connect people to destinations and experiences, with our great deals across our millions of hotels and holiday properties, flights, and experiences worldwide. No two days are the same at Agoda. Data and technology are at the heart of our culture, fueling our curiosity and innovation. If you’re ready to begin your best journey and help build travel for the world, join us. **We have only scratched the surface.** The online travel industry is an extremely dynamic and competitive space. Our aim is to deliver compelling designs that create a world\-class customer experience. You will have a key role in defining the future of our products that reach millions of customers. Despite our size, we operate in a startup\-like environment. **Work in the heart of Asia.** This is an amazing opportunity if you're looking for an exciting adventure abroad while expanding your international research capabilities. You get a chance to live in one of the most beautiful and most visited cities in the world! **This position is open to worldwide candidates and we will assist you with a generous relocation package, ensuring a smooth transition to working and living in Bangkok.** UX Researchers own end\-to\-end planning, execution, analysis and reporting of research projects, and consistently achieve research outputs needed by Product teams to inform strategic decisions. The research findings of UX Researchers impact Agoda's business success by informing Product teams regarding design or design process, end user experience, product direction, market strategy or similar. **Qualifications:** * Master Degree or equivalent experience in Computer Science, Human Computer Interaction, Information Science, Psychology, Social Science, or any related field * 7\+ years of relevant experience in consumer\-facing business domains * You are very comfortable with planning, scoping, conducting, analyzing and communicating research * Experience with Qualitative and user\-centered design methods (individual and group interviews, diary studies, direct observation, think\-aloud usability testing, etc.) * Experience with Quantitative research (metrics, survey design, response effects, sampling, crosstabs, and statistical concepts, etc.) * Experience in evaluating, negotiating, and working with external research vendors * Be able to describe user problems and business opportunities to a variety of stakeholders by leveraging both quantitative and qualitative data * You are able to demonstrate critical thinking beyond business requests * You are a great storyteller and communicator, able to speak fluently to business people, designers, engineers and other stakeholders (in English) * You enjoy collaborative work in a dynamic, data\-driven and creative environment **Responsibilities:** General Role Expectations: * Demonstrates critical thinking beyond explicit business requests * Functions independently in all operational research tasks * Leads core end\-to\-end research activities \- including driving research objectives, hypotheses, methodology, execution, analysis, and communication Research Skills: * Good judgement when evaluating research opportunities, e.g. importance, urgency, and impact for team * Ability to reasonably challenge details of research requests, and propose solid research solutions * Strong project execution with existing skills while constantly expanding his/her breadth and depth of abilities. * Knowledge of approaches for both quantitative and qualitative research, effectively triangulating findings, methods, and theories with others. * Application of tools and inspection methods to quantify the value and efficiency of research * Ability to adopt and adapt well\-established methods according to product/project situations, and innovate new methods in collaboration with more senior researchers * Knowledge of or ability to quickly learn key software tools common in the field (e.g. for surveys, diary studies, analysis) * Knowledge of data compliance and ethics in all of their activities. Collaboration \& Collaboration: * Drives cross\-discipline/department collaboration via research insights/activities. * Collaborates efficiently with other researchers and other disciplines, with clear commitments in case of shared ownership. * Demonstrates excellent communication skills; tailors communication style for different audiences, concise and to the point, open to taking input and feedback Research Community Contributions: * Contributes to the research community in one or more ways \- sharing work/ methodologies, coaching and/or mentoring other core or junior researchers, screening of Research candidates \#seattle \#sanfrancisco \#beijing \#newyork \#london \#bangkok \#singapore \#chicago \#hongkong \#singapore \#berlin \#shanghai \#losangeles \#boston \#austin \#toronto \#luxembourg \#munich \#oslo \#copenhagen \#stockholm \#helsinki \#brussels \#hamburg \#stuttgart \#taipei \#hamburg \#stuttgart \#dublin \#telaviv \#sydney \#melbourne \#perth \#vancouver \#montreal \#prague \#paris \#dublin \#telaviv \#milan \#rome \#tokyo \#osaka \#amsterdam \#moscow \#saintpetersburg \#frankfurt \#shenzhen \#istanbul \#dubai \#kiev \#seoul Please review our Hiring Process Guidelines before your interview — click here to learn how interviewing at Agoda works. **Discover More About Working At Agoda** * Agoda Careers https://careersatagoda.com * Facebook https://www.facebook.com/agodacareers/ * LinkedIn https://www.linkedin.com/company/agoda * YouTube https://www.youtube.com/agodalife **Equal Opportunity Employer** At Agoda, we pride ourselves on being a company represented by people of all different backgrounds and orientations. We prioritize attracting diverse talent and cultivating an inclusive environment that encourages collaboration and innovation. Employment at Agoda is based solely on a person’s merit and qualifications. We are committed to providing equal employment opportunity regardless of sex, age, race, color, national origin, religion, marital status, pregnancy, sexual orientation, gender identity, disability, citizenship, veteran or military status, and other legally protected characteristics. We will keep your application on file so that we can consider you for future vacancies and you can always ask to have your details removed from the file. For more details please read our privacy policy. **Disclaimer** We do not accept any terms or conditions, nor do we recognize any agency’s representation of a candidate, from unsolicited third\-party or agency submissions. If we receive unsolicited or speculative CVs, we reserve the right to contact and hire the candidate directly without any obligation to pay a recruitment fee.

title: Veterinary Practitioner - Small Animal - FTC (3 months)
description: **Veterinary Surgeon – Small Animal** ===================================== **Ark Vetcare, Dun Laoghaire** **Full\-time \\u007C Fixed Term Contract** **Salary up to €61,500 DOE (FTE)** **Start Date: June 2026** -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- **A Well\-Rounded GP Role with Real Support** --------------------------------------------- We’re looking for a Small Animal Veterinary Surgeon to join our team in Dun Laoghaire for a 3 month fixed\-term contract (from June to September. This is a genuine general practice role, offering a balanced mix of consulting, medical case work\-ups, and routine surgery. You’ll be joining a supportive, collaborative environment where there’s always someone to discuss cases with, while still having the autonomy to manage your own day. It’s a great opportunity for a vet who enjoys variety and wants to continue building confidence in both medicine and surgery. **Working Pattern That Supports Balance** ----------------------------------------- This is a full\-time position across five days per week, with a predictable and manageable rota. Typical hours are 7:30am to 4:30pm, with one later evening shift each week. Saturdays are worked on a 1 in 2 basis (8:00am–2:00pm), with a half day off in lieu the following week. There’s no out\-of\-hours or Sunday work, and just one bank holiday per year (9:00am–3:00pm), making this a role that genuinely supports a healthy work\-life balance. **What We’re Looking For** -------------------------- We’re seeking a VCI\-registered Veterinary Surgeon competent in first opinion practice. You’ll need to be confident in consultations, capable of working up medical cases, and comfortable with routine surgical procedures. Just as important is your attitude – we’re looking for someone who is a team player, communicates well, and brings a positive approach to their work. This role isn’t suited to a new graduate, as a level of independence in clinical decision\-making is required. **About Ark Vetcare Dun Laoghaire** ----------------------------------- Ark Vetcare is a well\-established veterinary group with three practices across South County Dublin. Our Dun Laoghaire clinic has been part of the community for over 50 years and has built a strong reputation for high\-quality care and long\-standing client relationships. While the building itself reflects its history, the clinical standards are modern and progressive. You’ll be working with a diverse and engaged client base who value the care we provide. The team in Dun Laoghaire includes six Veterinary Surgeons, four Veterinary Nurses, an Animal Care Assistant, and five client care team members. There’s a great mix of experience levels, and two certificate holders who are actively involved in mentoring and case collaboration. It’s a genuinely supportive practice where people enjoy working together, and where work\-life balance is taken seriously. **Benefits – Subject to Pro\-Rata** ----------------------------------- We offer a competitive salary, along with a comprehensive benefits package designed to support your wellbeing, development, and long\-term career. ### **Work Life Balance** 22 days Annual Leave Birthday Leave (paid day off on your birthday) ### **Focus on Employee Wellbeing** Paid Maternity Leave Package Paid Paternity Leave Cycle to Work Scheme Full access to EAP and Wellbeing Centre Paid Bereavement Leave Company Sick Pay ### **Development** €1300 annual CPD allowance with 5 days paid CPD days pro rata Funding of any Certificates / Diplomas / PhD’s you may want to pursue Career Progression Opportunities, both clinical and non\-clinical ### **Professional Memberships** VCI membership paid VDS cover paid ### **Additional Benefits** Annual Pay Reviews Dedicated CARE fund – annual contribution of €1,100 from IVC Evidensia for each Vet to use on a case of your choice to ensure successful outcome with your patient (T\&C’s apply) Pawesome Benefits \- reward gateway giving discounts and cashback with hundreds of retailers Employer Pension Contributions Free Flu Inoculations Discounted Veterinary Fees Opportunity to join company programmes (LGBTQ\+, Wellbeing Champions, Nurses Committee, Clinical Board and many more) **A Practice Where You Can Settle In and Grow** ----------------------------------------------- This is a great opportunity to join a well\-run, friendly practice where you’ll feel supported, valued, and part of the team from day one. If you’re looking for a solid GP role with variety, balance, and a genuinely positive working environment, we’d love to hear from you. Up to €61,500 DOE We may use artificial intelligence (AI) tools to support parts of the hiring process, such as reviewing applications, analyzing resumes, or assessing responses. These tools assist our recruitment team but do not replace human judgment. Final hiring decisions are ultimately made by humans. If you would like more information about how your data is processed, please contact us.

title: Wealth Management Executive
